advancedcomposites.description: Formed from some of the refined resources collected from mining ships, advanced composites is a general term for many compounds created to serve several purposes; most commonly used in the production of a variety of equipment parts.
advancedcomposites.name: Advanced Composites

advancedelectronics.description: Designed specifically to work alongside weapon and turret components, it is a variety of advanced electronics that allow different weapons and turrets to have a range of turning speeds, fire rates and cooldowns. Whereas field coils for shields and antimatter converters for engines are more modular and can be used 'across the board', different weapons and turrets expect different electronic systems to be used in order to ensure the correct properties. However, as all equipment is built and repaired on demand, all advanced electronics are shipped to shipyards and equipment docks together.
advancedelectronics.name: Advanced Electronics

antimattercells.description: Highly advanced magnetic storage devices that carry antimatter. Due to the effect of Hawking radiation and their being self-powered, antimatter cells cannot store antimatter indefinitely. They are produced and filled using refined hydrogen and primarily used in the production of engine parts, and also can be miniaturised to be used in claytronics.
antimattercells.name: Antimatter Cells

antimatterconverters.description: Attached to the antimatter cells used in engine parts for both main engines and thrusters, antimatter converters fine-tune the amount of energy used to a more specific configuration. It is the use of this component that allows so many variations of engine, as their number and set up greatly impact the power and efficiency of the final product.
antimatterconverters.name: Antimatter Converters

bomb_player_limpet_emp_01_mk1.description: A device that when triggered will cause a small electro-magnetic explosion without doing noticeable structural damage, this EMP is small enough to be fired from a hand-held weapon. It requires a precise aim to be most affective, as the pulse will only cripple very nearby computer and electronic systems.
bomb_player_limpet_emp_01_mk1.name: Spacesuit EMP

bomb_player_limpet_mine_01_mk1.description: Small enough to be launched from a hand-held weapon or similar, these explosives will stick to any magnetic surface and await to be triggered by the user. If positioned correctly, they can be used to destroy small parts of station and ship hull plating, but the user must be quite close for this to work, otherwise the bomb is likely to float away from its target before its magnet takes hold. Warning: Explosives can kill.
bomb_player_limpet_mine_01_mk1.name: Spacesuit Bomb

claytronics.description: More commonly known as programmable matter, claytronics are made up of millions of individual nanometre-scale computers known as catoms. This technology lives at the heart of any non-Xenon station, used to build anything from internal wiring to computer systems and control mechanisms - effectively everything but the hull of the station itself. Claytronics are always in high-demand, as the universe rebuilds itself after the Jump Gate shutdown.
claytronics.name: Claytronics

countermeasure_flares_01.description: A piece of equipment tried and tested over hundreds of years, the modern flare has continued to be a boon for many a combat pilot in recent years. Forcibly ejected from a compartment of a ship, flares then explode so that the sudden heat signature may distract a guided missile from its original target, and before exploding additionally send out a small electro-magnetic signature that can disrupt a target lock before a missile is even fired.
countermeasure_flares_01.name: Flares

dronecomponents.description: Much like weapon and turret components, drone components have been generalised and standardised across much of the Jump Gate network to be modularly used in all kinds of drone, making them a highly sought-after resource. Drone components are directly shipped to shipyards, equipment docks and stations to source drone-building, which is done on-demand as and when it is necessary.
dronecomponents.name: Drone Components

energycells.description: Contrary to common belief, Energy Cells are not simply glorified batteries; actually, they are sophisticated bio-chemical (or bio-mechanical, depending on technology) devices capable of storing energy near or at 100% efficiency.
energycells.name: Energy Cells

engine_arg_l_allround_01_mk1.description: Like most representative democracies, the Argon Federation prioritises crew survival in armed combat vessels over cost effectiveness. Since survivability is proportional to the ability to evade unwinnable situations, Argon engines feature powerful afterburners, providing ample boost for evasive manoeuvres, and the chance to return to the fight unscathed.nnShipowners who want to be ready for anything often choose the All-round class engine, which balances long-distance speed with localised acceleration capability.nnLarge, L-size engines may only be fitted to vessels of the corresponding size category.nnThe Mark I drive is the basic model.
engine_arg_l_allround_01_mk1.name: ARG L All-round Engine Mk1

engine_gen_spacesuit_01_mk1.description: Pilots using the standard spacesuit propulsion unit for extended distances are advised to have patience and an adequate supply of Oxygen.
engine_gen_spacesuit_01_mk1.name: Spacesuit Thrusters Mk1

engine_gen_spacesuit_01_mk2.description: It remains uncertain how the fashion for personal propulsion unit modifications first arose. However, the alarming increase in injuries from such "home-mods" led to the formalisation of standards for the Mk 2 spacesuit propulsion unit.nnThis widely applauded measure has radically reduced the accidental incineration rate across all known sectors.
engine_gen_spacesuit_01_mk2.name: Spacesuit Thrusters Mk2

engine_kha_m_combat_01_mk1.description: Although the underlying technology of Kha'ak engines remains mysterious to researchers, observed performance suggests a singular focus on combat effectiveness. Their propulsion systems seem to prioritise local manoeuvrability over long-distance travel capabilities, though the exact mechanisms driving these alien engines continue to elude understanding.nnMedium, M-size engines may only be fitted to vessels of the corresponding size category.
engine_kha_m_combat_01_mk1.name: KHA M Combat Engine Mk1

engine_kha_m_combat_02_mk1.description: Although the underlying technology of Kha'ak engines remains mysterious to researchers, observed performance suggests a singular focus on combat effectiveness. Their propulsion systems seem to prioritise local manoeuvrability over long-distance travel capabilities, though the exact mechanisms driving these alien engines continue to elude understanding.nnMedium, M-size engines may only be fitted to vessels of the corresponding size category.nnThe Kha'ak Engine Array appears to be an upgraded model, with improved specifications.
engine_kha_m_combat_02_mk1.name: KHA M Combat Engine Array Mk1

engine_kha_s_combat_01_mk1.description: Although the underlying technology of Kha'ak engines remains mysterious to researchers, observed performance suggests a singular focus on combat effectiveness. engine_par_l_allround_01_mk1.description: Paranid propulsion foundries have long been known for designing the fastest engines commercially available. With mathematics and engineering being a matter of religious determination and national pride, they've maintained their edge for countless decades. Paranid engines excel at both speed and long-range operations.nnShipowners who want to be ready for anything often choose the All-round class engine, which balances long-distance speed with localised acceleration capability.nnLarge, L-size engines may only be fitted to vessels of the corresponding size category.nnThe Mark I drive is the basic model.

With mathematics and engineering being a matter of religious determination and national pride, they've maintained their edge for countless decades. Paranid engines excel at both speed and long-range operations.nnThe Combat-class engine is optimised for high-burst delivery, and provides the acceleration and agility needed for battle situations.nnSmall, S-size engines may only be fitted to vessels of the corresponding size category.nnThis drive is usually available in three editions, Marks I, II and III, indicating the level of performance upgrade.

engineparts.description: Comprised of a number of different components that make up ship engines, engine parts are delivered straight to the end customer, most commonly shipyards and equipment docks, who then use them themselves to produce or repair engines. While naturally engine parts are a very necessary resource across the entire Jump Gate network, the ability to produce and repair engines on demand, instead of requiring an entirely separate production step for each, has greatly streamlined the universal economy.
engineparts.name: Engine Parts

fieldcoils.description: Used to fine-tune the arrays used on ship and station shielding, field coils allow for much more modulation than using shield components alone. As such shipyards have begun using them to build a much wider variety of shields than was previously available. Like many of the components used on ships - that aren't the hull itself - shield components are shipped straight to shipyards and equipment docks so that shields can be produced on-demand.
fieldcoils.name: Field Coils

foodrations.description: Food Rations come in a large variety; as far as rations for the species Homo sapiens are concerned, they mainly contain wheat, freeze-dried meat and different spices as well as vitamins and essential minerals.
foodrations.name: Food Rations

graphene.description: Produced from refined and heavily modified methane gas in the modern era, graphene is a semi-metal that has many uncommon properties. Due to its strength and resistance to heat and electricity, it is used to produce a variety of tech-level resources, and is also used to help in the reinforcement of hull parts.
graphene.name: Graphene

helium.description: A colourless, odourless and non-toxic noble gas, helium is found in great abundance in gas nebulas across the entire Jump Gate network and is usually collected by mining ships to be refined into superfluid coolant.
helium.name: Helium

hullparts.description: Made from refined metals or teladianium, Hull Parts are most commonly used to build the hulls of ships and stations, though they do have some other uses such as in drones and weapons. Usually layered for additional protection, and using other compounds to further reinforce, Hull Parts are still cheap to produce - a bonus, considering their position as the most frequently used resource in the Jump Gate network.
hullparts.name: Hull Parts

hydrogen.description: Historically, Hydrogen has been used mainly in H-fusion generators. These days however, with the rise of sustainable M/AM mass conversion, Hydrogen is routinely converted into anti-Hydrogen for use in Antimatter Cells.
hydrogen.name: Hydrogen

ice.description: H2O in its solid state of aggregation, used for industrial water supplies and general consumption after treatment.
ice.name: Ice

inv_advancedtargetingmodule.description: The Advanced Targeting Module provides a step up in a vessel's offensive capability.
inv_advancedtargetingmodule.name: Advanced Targeting Module

inv_agidevice_01.description: The Artificial General Intelligence processor is a poorly understood Xenon component, believed to provide AGI computing power.
inv_agidevice_01.name: AGI Processor

inv_agidevice_02.description: An AGI Heuristic Core is a CPU core which uses a heuristic approach to problem-solving. This means that it employs methods which are practical, though not guaranteed to be optimal, and which lead to the identification of a suitable approximation. Heuristics are commonly applied in Artificial Intelligence, and because of this, are also found in Xenon system architecture. Due to its status as a core technology in the creation of AGI Terraformers, and its potential for use in the manufacturing of powerful hacking devices, the AGI Heuristic Core is outlawed by most members of the Commonwealth.
inv_agidevice_02.name: AGI Heuristic Core

inv_agidevice_03.description: The Artificial General Intelligence Neural Network has so far defied explanation by the few Commonwealth scientists authorised to study it.nnIt is thought to perform, or perhaps merely support, a number of Xenon memory, sensing and communication functions.
inv_agidevice_03.name: AGI Neural Network

inv_algaescrubber.description: This life support enhancement uses a genetically modified form of algae as part of a biological waste recycling system.
inv_algaescrubber.name: Algae Scrubber

inv_antimatter_injection_sensor.description: This is a small device used in propulsion development to measure the flow and behaviour of antimatter particles in engine reaction chambers.
inv_antimatter_injection_sensor.name: Antimatter Injection Sensor

inv_aquaponicvegetable.description: Plant-based food produced in closed-cycle aquaponic systems, where nutrient-rich water from fish tanks sustains crops such as Spirulina and other edible plants. Widely used aboard stations and long-range vessels due to their reliability and low maintenance requirements.
inv_aquaponicvegetable.name: Aquaponic Vegetables

inv_argnusteak.description: Argnu steaks are a popular foodstuff derived from Argon bovines.
inv_argnusteak.name: Argnu Steak

inv_artificial_paranid_eye.description: The Orb of Abmanckulot resembles a precious jewel. However, it is in fact a prosthetic eye, once worn by Abmanckulot of the Honour Guard of Apotheosis.nnOcular prostheses are rare in Paranid society, as the loss of an eye usually results in social ostracism. In Abmanckulot's case, he suffered no such penalty and was, indeed, further honoured and promoted to Vice Admiral and the helm of a destroyer.nnThe prosthetic eye was a gift from the Pontifex himself, and rumour has it that it performs enhanced functions beyond simply replacing an eye.
inv_artificial_paranid_eye.name: Artificial Paranid Eye

inv_bandages.description: An essential part of any ship's first aid inventory.
inv_bandages.name: Bandages

inv_bionic_augmentation.description: Recent planetary experiments by the Segaris Pioneers have proven increasingly dangerous for scientists and volunteers alike. For that reason, it has been deemed necessary to employ body augmentations to improve the physical resistance of those who work in extreme environments.
inv_bionic_augmentation.name: Bionic Augmentation

inv_cahoonas.description: Produced from processed Argnu beef, Cahoona meat blocks are standardised food units formed using a mechanical press and enriched with Delexian wheat. Their high nutritional value and widespread consumption have made them a staple commodity across multiple systems.
inv_cahoonas.name: Cahoonas

inv_capacitor.description: Contemporary capacitor units leverage quantum entanglement to synchronise charge states between paired modules instantaneously, regardless of distance. Though originally developed for faster-than-light applications, this variant is primarily deployed in Harmonic Resonators.
inv_capacitor.name: Capacitor

inv_carbonfilter.description: The Carbon Filter is a graphene-based element used in life support systems.
inv_carbonfilter.name: Carbon Filter

inv_charged_particle_regulator.description: Used in particle science and weapon technologies, this regulator is capable of re-routing streams of charged particles millions of times a second. Doing so ensures maximal output with minimal overheating.
inv_charged_particle_regulator.name: Charged Particle Regulator

inv_crystal_01.description: First collected by an explorer from an asteroid in Ore Belt, Bandannite was named after the legendary Ban Danna, an Argon military commander and war hero before the Jump Gate shutdown. Rediscovered across the entire Jump Gate network after the realignment, this crystal is not the rarest, but will still fetch a good sum at the right dealer.
inv_crystal_01.name: Bandannite Crystal

inv_crystal_02.description: A crystal discovered at the heart of an asteroid in Kingdom End, Menelaene is named after the then princess of the Queendom of Boron, Menelaus. Many a miner have since found this rare crystal in asteroids scattered throughout space, and though many simply sell the crystals for a profit, some choose to hold onto the precious stone, in the hope that the Boron may one day re-emerge from the darkness.
inv_crystal_02.name: Menelaene Crystal

inv_crystal_03.description: Discovered by Ichirou Aguilar, a Terran mercenary trapped in the Jump Gate network after the Sol Gate was destroyed, this crystal is not found so commonly, but is also not so rare that its price on the free market is deemed ludicrous.
inv_crystal_03.name: Aguilite Crystal

inv_crystal_04.description: Named after the famed Boron scientist, Mi Ton, mitonene is rarely found among asteroids, at least in the current configuration of the Jump Gate network, therefore fetching a good price at most free market traders.
inv_crystal_04.name: Mitonene Crystal

inv_crystal_05.description: Though the pilot who first discovered this crystal wished to remain anonymous, and the reasons for its mysterious name are not known, it is confirmed to be one of the rarest precious stones currently to be found in the Jump Gate network. Traders and collectors will pay almost any price for it, giving a strong incentive for explorers to search asteroid fields closely for such a prize.
inv_crystal_05.name: Burnite Crystal

inv_crystalbinder.description: Industrial-grade paste formulated from refined Nividium compounds. Primarily employed in precision binding applications by jewellery manufacturers. Offers exceptional molecular stability and adhesion, particularly suited to crystalline materials.
inv_crystalbinder.name: Crystal Binder

inv_crystalbit.description: This drill bit component is formed from a hardened, crystalline composite, making it extremely durable.
inv_crystalbit.name: Crystal Bit

inv_damagedsingularityengine.description: The damaged remains of this particular Singularity Engine are in a better condition than most. Unlike most examples found to date, the core has not melted and it might be possible to get it working again with the right components. It's definitely going to need a new flux capacitor, however.
inv_damagedsingularityengine.name: Damaged Singularity Engine

inv_datafragment.description: Partial data segment of unknown origin. Can be decrypted or translated into Xenotext when analysed in conjunction with Xeno-Inscriptions. Commonly used in linguistic reconstruction and cipher mapping efforts.
inv_datafragment.name: Data Fragment

inv_decryptionmodule.description: A decryption module is a chip containing a series of decryption algorithms that can be used to bypass most computer firewalls. Though not much use on its own, it is the most important part of both the Defence and Security Decryption Systems, which makes it a vital and highly sought-after tool.
inv_decryptionmodule.name: Decryption Module

inv_digitalseminar_boarding.description: Available in many topics, digital seminars are perhaps the most comfortable way to learn on the go, making them very popular with learned experts and budding students alike. Sourced from many databases and universities across the Jump Gate network, a digital seminar allows you to learn a great deal about a given topic while not interrupting your daily routine. This has made them particularly popular in corporations where training is necessary but time is short.
inv_digitalseminar_boarding.name: Digital Seminar (Boarding)

inv_digitalseminar_engineering.description: Available in many topics, digital seminars are perhaps the most comfortable way to learn on the go, making them very popular with learned experts and budding students alike. Sourced from many databases and universities across the Jump Gate network, a digital seminar allows you to learn a great deal about a given topic while not interrupting your daily routine. This has made them particularly popular in corporations where training is necessary but time is short.
inv_digitalseminar_engineering.name: Digital Seminar (Engineering)

inv_digitalseminar_piloting.name: Digital Seminar (Piloting)

inv_electronics.description: Considered to be essential in the construction of most systems and subsystems, Electronics are basic components that are usable in a wide variety of electrical items.
inv_electronics.name: Electronics

inv_encrypted_contract.description: An Encrypted Contract cannot be accessed without verified authorisation.
inv_encrypted_contract.name: Encrypted Contract

inv_encrypted_espionagedocument.description: These documents were lifted from the Sandwell Archive servers. They contain a wide range of sensitive information which could potentially devastate the Argon Federation, if it were ever to fall into the wrong hands. The information is encrypted and currently inaccessible but, given enough time, a resourceful intelligence agency would surely be able to unlock it.
inv_encrypted_espionagedocument.name: Encrypted Espionage Documents

inv_encrypted_evidence.description: Often secreted in hard to find places, Encrypted Evidence contains secret documentation and must be protected with care.
inv_encrypted_evidence.name: Encrypted Evidence

inv_encrypted_malware.description: Used exclusively by malign actors, Encrypted Malware is an advanced programme used for espionage or electronic theft.
inv_encrypted_malware.name: Encrypted Malware

inv_encrypted_recording.description: Encrypted Recordings are generally sensitive surveillance records, which should not find their way into the wrong hands.
inv_encrypted_recording.name: Encrypted Recording

inv_encrypted_report.description: These encrypted reports are usually stored on ships' black boxes. They are used to log important transactions and other relevant metrics. However, due to either the state of the black box it was salvaged from or the encryption software used, it is unfortunately very rare to actually be able to access the report in any meaningful way.
inv_encrypted_report.name: Encrypted Report

inv_encrypted_researchpaper.description: Deep-technology analysts are occasionally tempted into prohibited arenas.nnEncrypted Research Papers are often suspected of relating to illegal and unethical research, for example into Artificial General Intelligence. They should be handled with care.
inv_encrypted_researchpaper.name: Encrypted Research Paper

inv_experimental_medicine.description: To account for the vastly differing immune systems of the Terran Protectorate's many offworld subjects, Sol's pharmaceutical industry continuously develops new methods to efficiently screen and immunise citizens in transit. This particular sample was prepared for injection via a pneumatically launched projectile.
inv_experimental_medicine.name: Experimental Vaccine

inv_familyheirloom.description: This artefact holds emotional value for a specific family.
inv_familyheirloom.name: Family Heirloom

inv_finemeal.description: While foodstuffs are reasonably abundant, some gourmands prefer a more elevated repast.
inv_finemeal.name: Fine Meal

inv_firstaidkit.description: The first aid kit is a standard-issue medical emergency resource, containing a range of dressings, disinfectants and basic medications.
inv_firstaidkit.name: First Aid Kit

inv_fluxcapacitor.description: The flux capacitor itself is a very old piece of technology, but for a long time it was little more than a curiosity with no viable practical application outside the laboratory. This changed with the development of the Singularity Engine, but since the other technologies used in this engine were a closely-guarded secret, now lost, flux capacitors are no longer in production.
inv_fluxcapacitor.name: Flux Capacitor

inv_glowmalt.description: A premium-grade malt derived from select microbrew varieties. Notable for its purity and potency, it significantly exceeds spacefuel in both quality and value, and is commonly used as a base ingredient in high-tier beverage formulations.
inv_glowmalt.name: Glow Malt

inv_hallucinogenics.description: The use of these powerful perception-distorting chemicals is ill-advised outside of a medical or scientific setting. Do not consume if piloting spacecraft or operating heavy machinery.
inv_hallucinogenics.name: Hallucinogenics

inv_harddrug_01.description: While this protein combinant has diverse physiological and psychological effects, it is universally considered to be dangerous to health and is illegal in most jurisdictions unless administered by a medical professional.
inv_harddrug_01.name: Delirium

inv_harmonicresonator.description: Incorporating trace amounts of a crystalline alloy refined from neutron star remnants, this device emits precisely tuned frequencies designed to modulate the ambient quantum field. Typically installed in nurseries and medical bays, it generates low-intensity ambient sounds associated with reduced neural stress responses.
inv_harmonicresonator.name: Harmonic Resonator

inv_head_of_split.description: The severed head of a male Split. In Split culture, it is not uncommon to scour the battlefield and take trophies to commemorate crucial victories. When another Split family has possession of such a trophy, it is a disgrace to all surviving family members of the defeated. This head was detached from the Zyarth Patriarchy officer Zar t'Qjjd, who headed the Fires of Defeat  offensive.
inv_head_of_split.name: Head of a Split

inv_herbicide.description: Derolax-OW is a highly toxic industrial herbicide. Gardeners are advised to use this only while wearing level 1 personal protective equipment.
inv_herbicide.name: Herbicide

inv_hotdogs.description: This jar of hotdogs appears to be quite old, but the seal is intact and the consumption safety indicator is green. There is some illegible handwriting on the lid, with a signature at the end that says something like "Outrider".
inv_hotdogs.name: Jar of "Cerberus" Brand Hotdogs

inv_hydraulicpump.description: This ancient technology remains in use due to its extreme reliability.
inv_hydraulicpump.name: Hydraulic Pump

inv_infusedbeverage.description: A high-grade beverage produced in zero-gravity, cold-infused with Massom Powder over a period of five jazuras. Variants differ in composition, but all exhibit mild restorative properties, including limited cellular regeneration and enhanced resistance to low-grade spaceborne pathogens.
inv_infusedbeverage.name: Infused Beverage

inv_interfaceunit.description: This interface unit is essentially an intelligent signal converter designed to allow different pieces of equipment, developed by different species and using a variety of technologies, to communicate. It has numerous input and output ports, and has a learning mode to allow it to determine the correct signals to use.
inv_interfaceunit.name: Interface Unit

inv_kyondevice_01.description: A poorly understood component in Kha'ak technology.
inv_kyondevice_01.name: Kyon Crystallite

inv_kyondevice_02.description: A poorly understood component in Kha'ak technology.
inv_kyondevice_02.name: Kyon Crystal Shard

inv_kyondevice_03.description: Used in the manufacture of Kha'ak beam weapons before the Jump Gate shutdown, many of these crystals were salvaged from wrecked Kha'ak ships during the Kha'ak War and Operation Final Fury, and so have been carried across the Jump Gate network or handed down over the generations. There are reports of it being found naturally occurring in some asteroids across the current Jump Gate network configuration, but so rarely is it found that these reports are yet to be confirmed.
inv_kyondevice_03.name: Kyon Crystal

inv_lostfragment.description: Believed to originate from an unknown civilisation - potentially a precursor to known species or an entirely undocumented alien culture - this object defies precise classification. While lacking apparent functional utility, its construction and provenance render it of significant interest to collectors and researchers alike.
inv_lostfragment.name: Lost Fragment

inv_magneticmaterial.description: Ingots of the mineral magnetite, which is a naturally occurring magnet.
inv_magneticmaterial.name: Lodestone

inv_majaglit.description: Majaglit is a type of jewelry seen as the mark of high-society by many Paranid. It is forged, usually, from the superheated shell of a maja snail, that is then crushed and heated again until it forms a glass-like liquid that when cooled will harden into whichever shape the artist has formed.
inv_majaglit.name: Majaglit

inv_massompowder.description: A processed food additive produced by the Split from Scruffin, a sweet root native to their territory. This yellow powder is traded widely and used by various races to enhance local foodstuffs. Some variants, blended with Soja Husk, are preferred by the Paranid.
inv_massompowder.name: Massom Powder

inv_microbrewdrink.description: Originally used to describe potent beverages produced in limited quantities by independent ventures, the term Microbrew Drink now refers to a broader category of locally brewed beverages. While most remain confined to their point of origin, select varieties have achieved wider recognition.
inv_microbrewdrink.name: Microbrew Drink

inv_microgimble.description: A microengineering component used mostly for stabilisation.
inv_microgimble.name: Micro-Gimbal

inv_micromotor.description: Used in advanced electrical systems, the micro-motor is resistant to electromagnetic interference.
inv_micromotor.name: Micro-Motor

inv_mixedfruit.description: A term applicable to a large variety of sweet produce.
inv_mixedfruit.name: Mixed Fruit

inv_modulartrigger.description: An experimental device of interest in automation engineering.
inv_modulartrigger.name: Modular Trigger

inv_nanite_fabricator.description: Given strict regulations on any self-replicating technology, nanites have hardly developed beyond being able to break down and restructure basic materials. Through minor adjustments, they can work to create circuitry, but the practice is often seen as both inefficient and potentially dangerous.
inv_nanite_fabricator.name: Nanite Fabricator

inv_needle.description: Sharp device used for precision penetration. Also in garment repair.
inv_needle.name: Needle

inv_neuralstimulator.description: This device employs quantum resonance fields to optimise neural pathway efficiency, resulting in enhanced cognitive processing and sensory perception across most sentient species. In many cases, it induces a meditative state. Up to ten units may be interlinked to enable a temporary shared consciousness network.
inv_neuralstimulator.name: Neural Stimulator

inv_nishala_oceanwater_sample.description: A sample of the ocean water present on the Boron homeworld of Nishala. These waters hold a spiritual significance for the Boron, as the womb of their civilisation.
inv_nishala_oceanwater_sample.name: Nishala Ocean Water Sample

inv_nividiumjewel.description: Following the discovery of a high-yield asteroid in 742 NT, bulk Nividium experienced a sharp decline in market value. In response, Teladi entrepreneurs developed a refinement process using ultra-high gravity compression to restructure the material into a dense crystalline form. Initially adopted as a status symbol by Paranid officials, Nividium Jewels have since gained traction among civilian elites.
inv_nividiumjewel.name: Nividium Jewel

inv_paranid_religious_tract.description: An arcane religious text that has been the source of rancorous Paranid theological debate ever since its adoption into canon.
inv_paranid_religious_tract.name: Paranid Religious Tract

inv_pricelessart.description: An artwork of significant cultural and monetary value.
inv_pricelessart.name: Priceless Art

inv_processing_panel_12G.description: The 12G Processing Panel Unit is an item of technology believed to support Xenon Artificial Intelligence. The 12G is measured and classified as an ambient superconductor. However, its internal complexity indicates it may have additional functions.
inv_processing_panel_12G.name: Processing Panel Unit 12G

inv_processing_panel_19L.description: The Xenon 19L Processing Panel Unit's function is mysterious and likely to form part of their Artificial Intelligence capability. It's anterior concave element may be a connector to other known and hypothesised PPUs.
inv_processing_panel_19L.name: Processing Panel Unit 19L

inv_processing_panel_46B.description: The Xenon Processing Panel Unit 46B exhibits deep memory storage capacity, though it is not yet understood how this can be accessed or, indeed, utilised.nnIt is likely that it interacts with other 46B PPUs and can be connected in series, though researchers refuse to preclude the possibility that such an experiment could "wake up" a Xenon instance in the laboratory and potentially take over the hosting vessel or station.
inv_processing_panel_46B.name: Processing Panel Unit 46B

inv_processing_panel_7A.description: The 7A Processing Panel Unit is a Xenon artefact with enigmatic characteristics. Scientific debate is not settled on the matter. However, authorised investigators suggest it may be a centre of creative intelligence and an important element of Xenon adaptability.
inv_processing_panel_7A.name: Processing Panel Unit 7A

inv_processing_panel_8X.description: While laboratory investigation suggests the Xenon Processing Panel Unit 8X is an excellent energy store, so little is understood about its functions that it has only ever been tested in isolated conditions.nnSome theorise that it may form part of a Xenon ganglion, or subsidiary "microbrain".
inv_processing_panel_8X.name: Processing Panel Unit 8X

inv_produce.description: Premium produce cultivated aboard orbital plantations. Engineered for high nutritional yield across diverse species, these foodstuffs are grown under multispectral lighting and include both heritage and hybrid varieties. When combined with Massom Powder and vacuum-sealed, these edibles retain full freshness for up to 37 mazuras.
inv_produce.name: Produce

inv_programmablefieldarray.description: Engineers developing new forms of spatial fields normally start out using a programmable field array. This allows them to modify the size, shape and composition of the field, but at a much lower power output than would normally be available in a final production model.
inv_programmablefieldarray.name: Programmable Field Array

inv_quantum_data_shard.description: This Data Shard appears to contain old Xenon code and data. Some speculate that it could be used to research Xenon technologies, but it would require advanced research facilities and highly skilled research staff to unlock its secrets. This shard may also be sold on the black market, as its casing is made from valuable materials.
inv_quantum_data_shard.name: AGI Quantum Data Shard

inv_rapidsandfilter.description: An ancient and reliable technology, this life support subsystem only operates correctly in the presence of gravity or pseudo-gravity within defined tolerances.
inv_rapidsandfilter.name: Rapid Sand Filter

inv_rareartefact.description: Recovered from an unknown origin, this artefact fragment has been encased for preservation and display. While no practical function has been identified, its atypical material composition and unclassified provenance make it a sought-after decorative piece in private residences and public venues.
inv_rareartefact.name: Rare Artifact

inv_remotedetonator.description: A remote detonator can be attached to most explosives in order to ensure that they only go off when the user wishes, minimising the risk of accidental or pre-emptive detonations.
inv_remotedetonator.name: Remote Detonator

inv_salvagedelectronics.description: Salvaged electronics can be recycled into new components.
inv_salvagedelectronics.name: Salvaged Electronics

inv_sandwell_archive_pass.description: This pass allows its holder to enter the relay server room hosted by the Antigone Republic, and gives access to the Argon Federation's Sandwell Archive. It will not hold up to intense scrutiny, however, since it is a counterfeit fabricated by the Terran Secret Service.
inv_sandwell_archive_pass.name: Sandwell Archive Pass

inv_sealed_vacuum_tubes.description: Hazard-ready containers like these play a vital part in the transferral of organic substances between Sol's many stations and settlements. This particular batch seems to have suffered abrasions, but the crest of the Martian megalopolis Twelvetowns remains clearly visible on one of the undamaged surfaces.
inv_sealed_vacuum_tubes.name: Sealed Vacuum Tube

inv_securecontainer.description: A vacuum-sealed container that required a six-digit code both to open and shut. These containers do not have the same integrity as a ship container, but as long as they are not tampered with, will securely hold whatever is placed inside for a considerable time.
inv_securecontainer.name: Secure Container

inv_securitybypasssystem.description: The Security Bypass System is a hardened malware module used by those with ill intent to access systems without detection.
inv_securitybypasssystem.name: Security Bypass System

inv_securitydecryptionsystem.description: A Security Decryption System will give a user access to all panels in the Engineering Section and all panels in the Security Office, except for the Turret Control Panel.nnSecurity Decryption Systems are beloved on the black market as the catch-all approach to circumventing or controlling computer and electronic systems. With heuristic algorithms and enough storage to save very few successful and failed hacking attempts, the security decryption system is capable of learning from its past mistakes and changing its approach until it is able to break the system. Experts have confirmed that these systems are not AGI, but that they do come worryingly close.
inv_securitydecryptionsystem.name: Security Decryption System

inv_securityslicer.description: A Security Slicer, when used on a Turret Control Panel, will penetrate a station's defensive systems and shut down turrets for a limited amount of time.nnAs a direct evolution of other hacking tools, it is usually harder to come by. Since it can leave a station critically vulnerable, it is highly illegal to carry one of these devices on your person.
inv_securityslicer.name: Security Slicer

inv_sedative.description: An advanced sedative used in medical treatments.
inv_sedative.name: Sedative

inv_segarian_research.description: Analysing the Headquarters' databases, Boso found and collected Segarian research on Terraforming. This file contains, but is not limited to: Preliminary Studies, Empirical Findings, Engineering Blueprints, Control Software Code and Quantitative Data.
inv_segarian_research.name: Qualitative Research on Segarian Terraforming

inv_seminar_management_0.description: Available in many topics, digital seminars are perhaps the most comfortable way to learn on the go, making them very popular with learned experts and budding students alike. Sourced from many databases and universities across the Jump Gate network, a digital seminar allows you to learn a great deal about a given topic while not interrupting your daily routine. This has made them particularly popular in corporations where training is necessary but time is short.
inv_seminar_management_0.name: Basic Seminar (Management)

inv_sensitivedatapacket.description: The owner of this data packet required the information in it to remain private.
inv_sensitivedatapacket.name: Sensitive Data Packet

inv_snailshells.description: Calcareous remnants of the Maja Snail, these shells are the primary raw material used in the production of Majaglit. Their iridescent composition is valued for applications in high-grade ornamentation and precision-crafted jewellery.
inv_snailshells.name: Snail Shells

inv_spacedyepizza.description: A multi-layered culinary construct distinguished by a crust patterned with iridescent spectral gradients. Favoured by species with an appreciation for complex, layered compositions, Space-Dye Pizza has gained notable popularity in niche gastronomic circles.
inv_spacedyepizza.name: Space-Dye Pizza

inv_spaceflycaviar.description: A delicacy among the Split and enjoyed by other races also, spacefly caviar is made from collected spacefly eggs and preservatives, stored in airtight containers and often enjoyed with alcoholic beverages. Due to the modern rarity of the spacefly, this caviar is harder to produce and more difficult to come by, and recently its price has begun to rise.
inv_spaceflycaviar.name: Spacefly Caviar

inv_spaceflyeggs.description: Laid by spaceflies in the latter stages of their development, spacefly eggs have long been collected, particularly by the Split, for many uses, among them to be turned into spacefly caviar or to be frozen and turned into jewelry. With spaceflies becoming rarer in recent years, the price of their eggs has recently skyrocketed.
inv_spaceflyeggs.name: Spacefly Eggs

inv_split_ceremonial_dagger.description: Ceremonial Daggers hold an important place in the Split warrior culture. The first ones were awarded as a badge of honour to the brave Split warriors who claimed new systems for Emperor Thuruk, Patriarch of all Split. This dagger was bestowed upon the honourable warriors of the Zyarth Patriarchy who drove the Argon nuisance out of Split space and claimed it for their Patriarch.
inv_split_ceremonial_dagger.name: Split Ceremonial Dagger

inv_timewarp.description: The Singularity Engine Time Accelerator, or SETA, has fallen out of common use in recent times, as the necessary components required to build the device were lost or destroyed during the Jump Gate shutdown. If a pilot is able to find one of these lost singularity engines, however, it may be possible to build a personal device that functions similarly to the old-style ship attachment, compressing time around the attached spacesuit and deactivating with any major interference to the spacesuit's stability. No known side effects of using SETA exist, but they cannot be ruled out.
inv_timewarp.name: SETA

inv_unmarked_black_box.description: Flight data recording device. Often not black.
inv_unmarked_black_box.name: Unmarked Black Box

inv_xenoinscriptions.description: Non-human symbols of verified extraterrestrial origin. The source civilisation remains unknown, but the inscriptions are of interest to intelligence agencies. Comparative analysis has occasionally enabled breakthroughs in alien cipher systems, offering strategic advantages in counterintelligence.
inv_xenoinscriptions.name: Xeno-Inscription

inv_xenoterran_implant.description: A lovingly crafted, if slightly unusual, piece of hardware, to be inserted between the artificial skin layer and the stem connector of a Yaki cybernetics set. Apart from being visually stunning, this particular design also features a prototype command module with an internal switch, as well as an adrenaline injector.
inv_xenoterran_implant.name: Xenoterran Implant

inv_xenotext.description: Compiled outputs resulting from the deciphering of Data Fragments. Content varies, including incomplete cultural records and obsolete technical schematics. Primarily of archival interest, though some instances have been reclassified under strategic intelligence protocols. Commonly traded among academic institutions, collectors, and private intelligence entities.
inv_xenotext.name: Xenotexts

khaakalloy.description: A highly versatile carbon allotrope noted for its structural integrity and unique electromagnetic properties. Though difficult to synthesise, it is widely employed across the entire Kha'ak technology stack. Salvaged Kha'ak wreckage typically yields recoverable Allographyne Scrap, which can be reprocessed into fragments and integrated into advanced manufacturing workflows.
khaakalloy.name: Allographyne

khaakscrapmetal.description: Typically reprocessed from salvaged Allographyne Scrap, these carbon allotrope fragments are valued for both their structural integrity and electromagnetic properties. They are utilised in a host of after-market modifications, repairs, and manufacturing workflows.
khaakscrapmetal.name: Allographyne Fragments

majadust.description: Chemically processed from crushed maja snail shells, maja dust is a relaxant and hallucinogenic sold as a powder-substance that is inhaled. Though any official Paranid government would have the populous believe that maja dust is not commonly consumed, it is thought to be highly likely, particularly in light of the ongoing Paranid civil war, that many Paranid and non-Paranid alike are partaking in the outlawed substance.
majadust.name: Maja Dust

majasnails.description: Grown across many Paranid colonies, as well as in tanks across Paranid space, maja snails provide Paranid with a good number of the nutrients they need to survive. Commonly treated very well, once a snail dies its shell and flesh are both processed as part of soja husk, along with soja beans. The shell of the maja snail is also commonly used as the main ingredient of the drug known as maja dust.
majasnails.name: Maja Snails

meat.description: Though meat comes in a great variety, the most sought-after type as of late is so-called 'vegan meat', synthesised and cloned not from real animals, but from cell cultures. However, real Argnu meat is still popular within the human population of many worlds.
meat.name: Meat

medicalsupplies.description: Medical supplies contain a number of different concoctions and remedies used to keep station workforce healthier and happier during their time aboard. Completely natural in design, these supplies have been vetted by both the Argon Federation and Godrealm of the Paranid to ensure they are both legal and safe, and thus there is no licence necessary to carry or supply them.
medicalsupplies.name: Medical Supplies

methane.description: Found in ample supply in gas nebulas across the Jump Gate network, methane (CH4) is collected by mining ships and taken to refineries to be processed into graphene.
methane.name: Methane

microchips.description: Used in a wide variety of equipment parts, micro-chips are produced using silicon wafers, which, while fragile, allows them to conduct at a much higher rate. This, in turn, allows far better processing in the equipment that uses the micro-chips, which includes many advanced electronics and components.
microchips.name: Microchips

missile_cluster_heavy_mk1.description: As the name implies, a cluster-based system foregoes the powerful single main charge for a means of releasing several smaller explosives simultaneously, potentially damaging multiple targets at once. While the damage caused to each individual target is naturally significantly less than could be achieved with a unitary charge, cluster-munitions, packing sufficiently powerful sub-munitions, can provide a pilot with the means to quickly disable a hostile target by taking out key components.nnLike the dumbfire-type munitions, the cluster-based weapon does not possess the means to compensate for changes in the target's trajectory. Target data from the ship's onboard computer systems is used to program a timer moments before the weapon's launch. Once the timer reaches its end, ideally moments before the missile impacts, a plethora of smart mini-bombs or "bomblets" are dispersed around the target area. These feature fairly advanced homing software to enable them to seek out the more vulnerable parts of the exposed systems of an enemy combatant.nnThe Heavy Cluster weapon represents a considerable step up in terms of destructive capability, while sacrificing thrust-to-weight ratio, compared to its smaller counterpart. An increase in the number and size of bomblets, along with a new and improved explosive compound, results in significantly increased effectiveness.
missile_cluster_heavy_mk1.name: Heavy Cluster Missile Mk1

missile_cluster_light_mk1.description: As the name implies, a cluster-based system foregoes the powerful single main charge for a means of releasing several smaller explosives simultaneously, potentially damaging multiple targets at once. These feature fairly advanced homing software to enable them to seek out the more vulnerable parts of the exposed systems of an enemy combatant.
missile_cluster_light_mk1.name: Light Cluster Missile Mk1

missile_dumbfire_heavy_mk1.description: Although more powerful than the lighter dumbfire weapons, the Heavy Dumbfire missile is still an economical choice of weapon which allows pilots to punch significantly above their weight. A compromise on thruster performance allows for a significantly enhanced warhead.nnDeployed from either a Mk1 or Mk2 standard launcher, a gas-driven piston ejects the missile from the launch tube, after which the thruster engages once safe separation is confirmed. This combination provides enhanced safety, prevents accidental burn on a jammed weapon, and significantly reduces maintenance overheads.nnLike its cheaper and less powerful counterpart, the missile utilises a simple impact fuse and an additional fallback timer. However, the Heavy Dumbfire missile features two separate warhead components that sit in tandem directly behind the impact fuse. When triggered, the precursor warhead immediately detonates, sending a superheated dart capable of piercing the outer hull of most ships. Microseconds after the precursor is fired, the much larger main charge triggers a conventional high explosive, causing potentially catastrophic damage to smaller vessels and significant damage to larger ones. If the weapon fails to impact the intended target, it will continue in a straight line until the onboard timer expires, at which point it will safely self-destruct.
missile_dumbfire_heavy_mk1.name: Heavy Dumbfire Missile Mk1

missile_dumbfire_heavy_mk2.description: Although more powerful than the lighter dumbfire weapons, the Heavy Dumbfire missile is still an economical choice of weapon which allows pilots to punch significantly above their weight. If the weapon fails to impact the intended target, it will continue in a straight line until the onboard timer expires, at which point it will safely self-destruct.nnImprovements to the thruster chemical composition and the use of a more advanced type of fuel means that the Heavy Dumbfire Mk2 is a slightly more responsive weapon system compared to its predecessor.
missile_dumbfire_heavy_mk2.name: Heavy Dumbfire Missile Mk2

missile_dumbfire_light_mk1.description: Commonly referred to as the "Wasp" among the crews of Argon ships, this rocket system is fitted with a target-data processor instead of the technologically advanced guidance systems found on more expensive weapons. As a non-guided weapon, it does not possess the means to compensate for changes in the target's trajectory. While use against agile targets is therefore not advised, when used against slower targets the Wasp allows pilots to punch significantly above their weight without having to invest in more expensive missile-based weapons.nnDeployed from either a Mk1 or Mk2 standard launcher, a gas-driven piston ejects the missile from the launch tube, after which the thruster engages once safe separation is confirmed. This combination provides enhanced safety, prevents accidental burn on a jammed weapon, and significantly reduces maintenance overheads. The Light Dumbfire missile features a simple impact fuse, which triggers the warhead upon confirmed impact with any object, and an additional fallback timer. The timer is a solution to the problem that any projectile fired into space will eventually make contact and cause significant damage to whatever it impacts with. Upon reaching the specified time after launch, if the target has not already been hit, the weapon will self-detonate, leaving behind harmless microparticles as its only debris.
missile_dumbfire_light_mk1.name: Light Dumbfire Missile Mk1

missile_dumbfire_light_mk2.description: Commonly referred to as the "Wasp" among the crews of Argon ships, this rocket system is fitted with a target-data processor instead of the technologically advanced guidance systems found on more expensive weapons. The increase in performance over the cheaper version can make the difference between victory and emergency ejection.
missile_dumbfire_light_mk2.name: Light Dumbfire Missile Mk2

missile_emp_mk1.description: Known by some pilots as the Hamstring, the EMP is a highly specialised, vessel-disabling missile system frequently used by law enforcement ships for non-lethal interdiction missions. With a powerful matter/antimatter reaction engine, and a highly specialised warhead, this weapon can reach top speeds well in excess of that of common ship travel drives. When it reaches its target, it sheds the protective outer shell and exposes an elaborate antenna array, which it uses to accurately aim and discharge a powerful electromagnetic pulse. The effect of this pulse is a prompt shutdown of the target ship's travel drive, which makes the law enforcement vessel's task of catching up significantly easier. Using a combination of extremely accurate radar, and computer-based prediction algorithms carried out before launch, the weapon works by predicting an impact point from a straight line approach rather than attempting to chase down the target via curves and turns like most other weapons. It is worth noting that, with the extreme speeds provided by the Peregrine engine system, the Hamstring can be a difficult beast to control.
missile_emp_mk1.name: EMP Missile Mk1

missile_gen_l_dumbfire_01_mk1.description: The Heavy Cluster Rocket system hearkens back to the simpler days of the 25th century, when the delicate dance of ever improving target acquisition and electronic countermeasures was trampled by the brute force of unguided explosive barrages. To this day, the majority of missile interception systems are optimised against torpedoes and guided missiles, not clusters of rockets. Hitting anything faster than a station can still be a challenge though, forcing a delivery from close range.
missile_gen_l_dumbfire_01_mk1.name: Heavy Cluster Rocket

missile_gen_l_guided_01_mk1.description: It was during initial testing that a curious side-effect of a new thruster nozzle would give the "Banshee" missile its now iconic nickname. While undergoing power and stability testing, the weapon was fixed in a rig while the rocket motor was fired. The research team found that, at certain settings, the weapon emitted a sound wave that bore an eerie resemblance to the screams of the Banshee, a Terran mythical creature. This issue was eventually tracked down to an improperly configured pressure valve, but by the time a fix had been issued, the name had stuck.nnCompared to the Light Guided missiles, the Banshee is significantly slower but carries an absolutely devastating punch. In small salvoes, this is a good weapon to install on a fighter intended to counter heavier corvettes, and potentially even frigates, where the inherent agility advantage of the fighter can help offset the somewhat sluggish nature of this weapon. An upgraded weapon-to-ship data link not only improves the chances of first time contact, but also allows the missile to come around for multiple passes in the event of failure to connect on the first attempt. However, the weapon can be very susceptible to even basic heat-based decoys.
missile_gen_l_guided_01_mk1.name: Heavy Guided Missile

missile_gen_m_dumbfire_01_mk1.description: Although more powerful than fighter-based dumbfire weapons, this turret-based rocket remains an economical choice, allowing captains to punch significantly above their weight. As a non-guided weapon, it lacks the means to compensate for changes in a target's trajectory, flying instead on a straight path. Compared to its smaller cousin, a proportionally larger fuel source extends its range and compensates for a heavier warhead.nnThe explosive force of the warhead is large enough to strike entire groups of surface elements, but the fuse is too simplistic to include any form of proximity detection. Capital ships and stations are, therefore, the intended recipients.
missile_gen_m_dumbfire_01_mk1.name: Medium Dumbfire Rocket

missile_gen_m_dumbfire_01_mk2.description: Commonly referred to as the "Wasp" among the crews of Argon ships, this rocket system is fitted with a target-data processor instead of the technologically advanced guidance systems found on more expensive weapons. As a non-guided weapon, it does not possess the means to compensate for changes in the target's trajectory. While use against agile targets is therefore not advised, when used against slower targets the Wasp allows pilots to punch significantly above their weight without having to invest in more expensive missile-based weapons.nnThe Breaching variant uses a ceramics-sheathed warhead with a modulated charge to achieve partial shield-piercing characteristics. The financial wisdom of using such complex and expensive materials on a budget rocket may be questionable, but its humbling impact is not.
missile_gen_m_dumbfire_01_mk2.name: Medium Breaching Rocket

missile_gen_m_guided_01_mk1.description: Like its smaller cousin, the turret-based Medium Guided Missile is a popular compromise in cost, target tracking, and lethality. Despite its larger size, it is still nimble enough to chase after small fighters, and still cheap enough to waste it on long-range deterrence at the edge of the engagement envelope.nnAn upgraded weapon-to-ship data link not only improves the chances of first time contact, but also allows the missile to come around for multiple passes in the event of failure to connect on the first attempt. However, the weapon can be very susceptible to even basic heat-based decoys.
missile_gen_m_guided_01_mk1.name: Medium Guided Missile

missile_gen_m_smart_01_mk1.description: The Medium Smart Missile was an ATF project intended to complement the smaller and slightly more agile Light Smart system. Where the Light Smart missile would be used against the agile fighters and heavy fighters of the Xenon, the Medium Smart Missile was meant for corvette-sized and larger ships. Like its smaller counterpart, the Medium Smart Missile also required the same focus on friend-foe discrimination capability, but its warhead would be scaled up in order to more effectively handle the larger types of targets it was intended for use against. Unfortunately, the weapon system suffered the same fate as the Light Smart missile; the ATF stopped development in favour of more promising weapon systems and the technology remained dormant for many decades before research was continued by an intrepid Argon scientist, who obtained the schematics from an undercover agent.
missile_gen_m_smart_01_mk1.name: Medium Smart Missile

missile_gen_s_dumbfire_01_mk1.description: Commonly referred to as the "Wasp" among the crews of Argon ships, this rocket system is fitted with a target-data processor instead of the technologically advanced guidance systems found on more expensive weapons. missile_gen_s_dumbfire_01_mk1.name: Light Dumbfire Rocket

When it reaches its target, it sheds the protective outer shell and exposes an elaborate antenna array, which it uses to accurately aim and discharge a powerful electromagnetic pulse. missile_gen_s_emp_01_mk1.name: Light Guided EMP Missile

missile_gen_s_guided_01_mk1.description: The Light Guided missile provides the first step into the world of guided missiles for most rookie pilots. It is a relatively inexpensive system compared to its more capable counterparts. Cheap, fairly easy to produce, and with very little in the way of training required for its use, this is a great way to give a new pilot a significant advantage in combat.nnThe guidance system is a simplistic one-way data link that enables the launch platform to continuously feed the missile updated information on its position relative to that of its intended target. Unfortunately, it is exceptionally easy to jam and, combined with the use of a very rudimentary thermal imaging sensor, it is very poorly suited to engaging agile targets with capable missile countermeasures on board. In keeping with the other cost-saving measures of the weapon, the Light Guided Mk1 has only a very basic ability to penetrate the armoured hulls of warships. However, if a breach does occur, the fact that the charge was originally designed for mining in deep space means it retains a high degree of efficiency.
missile_gen_s_guided_01_mk1.name: Light Guided Missile

missile_gen_s_heatseeker_01_mk1.description: The Light Heatseeker missile, known by many pilots as the Stormbolt, is the Paranid answer to the need for a missile system with minimal prelaunch lock-on time. Given the often tightly choreographed Paranid approach to combat manoeuvres, and the frantic nature of dogfights in general, where split-second decisions and actions can have severe consequences, this targeting logic was deemed in accordance with the Holy Three-Dimensionality. Funding for its development was allocated, and what they got in return was a weapon that has virtually no lock-on time requirement whatsoever. That advantage, however, comes at a significant cost to threat-identification and countermeasure burn-through capability. In practice, this means that the weapon is easily foiled by decoys and best used either in massed volleys, often from multiple ships, or in drawn-out engagements in which the enemy's supply of countermeasures is depleted. Naturally, this sort of approach does not sit too well with a military that prides itself on efficiency.nnExactly how the blueprints for the Stormbolt found their way to the open markets is unknown, but while the weapon failed to gain traction with the Paranid, other races with a less frugal view on munitions expenditure have since made good use of it.
missile_gen_s_heatseeker_01_mk1.name: Light Heatseeker Missile

missile_gen_s_smart_01_mk1.description: The Light Smart missile was an ATF project looking into advanced seeker technologies, intended to enable the use of missiles against swarms of Xenon craft, even in protracted battles with friendlies in close proximity. This necessitated highly advanced friend-foe identification capabilities, as well as a tightly restrained explosive effector, in order to avoid accidentally inflicting damage on friendly ships near the intended target. ATF stopped development in favour of more promising weapon systems and the technology remained dormant for many decades before research was continued by an intrepid Argon scientist, who obtained the schematics from an undercover agent.nnThe Light Smart missile relies on a special chemical mixture for its burn that gives a markedly reduced thermal signature. This, combined with the smokeless launch of the magnetic induction system, means that its launch can be very difficult to detect by the intended target, especially if the pilot is clever enough to conceal the launch with an appropriate manoeuvre. The Light Smart missile has a very small warhead; however, once released, its long, narrow dart is capable of penetrating through the forms of most Xenon ships dealing massive damage to their internals.
missile_gen_s_smart_01_mk1.name: Light Smart Missile

missile_gen_s_swarm_01_mk1.description: The Light Swarm missile is the Paranid solution to the need for a missile system with an enhanced defence bypass capability from a single launcher system. A single container missile is launched from a standardised universal launcher tube. The container is really only there to ensure compatibility with standardised tube dimensions, and is discarded immediately after clearing the launch-tube. As the four-part outer shell peels away, the Light Swarm missile reveals its many, viciously fanged heads in the form of several smaller, independently operating missiles that streak towards the target.nnAn unfortunate compromise born of the smaller form factor of these sub-missiles, is naturally that their warheads and guidance sections are not nearly as capable as those of a full-sized weapon. However, where a full-sized weapon would present a significantly easier interception task for the defensive gun and missile systems on a target vessel, the multiple smaller weapons complicate this, both by increasing the saturation level through sheer numbers but also by being smaller and effectively a harder target to hit. This requires guns to stay longer on target, and defensive missile banks to expend many more shots to avert an impact.
missile_gen_s_swarm_01_mk1.name: Light Swarm Missile

missile_gen_s_torpedo_01_mk1.description: Torpedoes are significantly slower than other missile weapons but they provide far and away more firepower on impact than any other type. They are best utilised, therefore, against less agile ships and emplacements that cannot easily avoid the impact simply by moving out of the way. The Light Torpedo missile is a hot-launched weapon. This means the rocket motor fires immediately upon button-press and begins its slow, ponderous journey towards the target. This in turn imparts significantly more stress and maintenance costs upon the launcher itself. With an enlarged launcher comes a larger body which allows significantly more room for explosive mass in the weapon. This torpedo is a two-stage weapon with a powerful high explosive precursor warhead that violently rips a hole in the enemy vessel. A primary charge then detonates and turns the weapon into a massive and extremely damaging fragmentation grenade, more than powerful enough to rip apart most ships in a single blow. The Light Torpedo missile is extremely resilient to countermeasures.
missile_gen_s_torpedo_01_mk1.name: Light Torpedo

missile_heatseeker_heavy_mk1.description: After evaluating the performance of the Light Heatseeker missile, the design-team responsible for its creation noticed something peculiar. During testing, a significant number of encounters resulted in mission failures that could have been prevented by fitting the missile with just a slightly more powerful warhead. Unfortunately, when the team brought this matter up, politics got in the way and what was intended to be a simple upgrade turned into a weapon so different that it became its own development program entirely.nnThis new Heavy Heatseeker weapon, nicknamed the Icarus, is a heat-seeking missile with a powerful warhead capable of dealing significant, possibly even catastrophic, damage to most fighters, heavy fighters and corvettes. While multiple impacts may be required to take out a frigate, the weapon is more than capable of that as well. It is not too agile though and like its smaller cousin, it suffers from the same design compromises on its guidance package, meaning it can be fairly easily defeated by decoys.
missile_heatseeker_heavy_mk1.name: Heavy Heatseeker Missile Mk1

missile_swarm_heavy_mk1.description: The bigger sister to the Light Swarm missile, the Heavy Swarm version is also a Paranid weapon system designed to overcome defensive anti-missile measures on a well equipped target. Like its smaller counterpart, the Heavy Swarm missile uses a two-stage solution to achieve this. A larger primary stage consists of an external shell that carries multiple, smaller missiles internally. The outer shell ensures compliance with standardised launchers, and the internally carried sub-missiles are released just a few seconds after launch. At that point, they disperse and follow an infra-red spectrum guidance laser from the launching ship to the intended target. The use of the laser makes the system somewhat cheaper, which helps offset the cost of several guidance sections, but also makes the weapon susceptible to infra-red decoys such as flares.nnThe sub-missiles are fitted with fairly powerful high-explosive warheads behind a teladianium disc. When triggered, the teladianium forms into a shape somewhat like a dart. This dart penetrates into the target where it fractures into large, devastating shards of almost molten metal, causing tremendous damage.
missile_swarm_heavy_mk1.name: Heavy Swarm Missile Mk1

As the four-part outer shell peels away, the Light Swarm missile reveals its many, viciously fanged heads in the form of several smaller, independently operating missiles that streak towards the target.nnAn unfortunate compromise born of the smaller form factor of these sub-missiles, is naturally that their warheads and guidance sections are not nearly as capable as those of a full-sized weapon. However, where a full-sized weapon would present a significantly easier interception task for the defensive gun and missile systems on a target vessel, the multiple smaller weapons complicate this, both by increasing the saturation level through sheer numbers but also by being smaller and effectively a harder target to hit. This requires guns to stay longer on target, and defensive missile banks to expend many more shots to avert an impact.
missile_swarm_light_mk1.name: Light Swarm Missile Mk1

missile_torpedo_heavy_mk1.description: The Heavy Torpedo missile is more commonly known as the Triton. Torpedoes are significantly slower than other missile weapons but they provide far and away more firepower on impact than any other type. They are best utilised, therefore, against less agile ships and emplacements that cannot easily avoid the impact simply by moving out of the way. Significantly more powerful than the Light Torpedo missile, this weapon is the premier choice against the heaviest and most durable of hostile vessels. No other weapon delivers as much destructive power as the Triton in a single explosive blast. The Triton missile is a hot-launched weapon. This means the rocket motor fires immediately upon button-press and begins its slow, ponderous journey towards the target. This in turn imparts significantly more stress and maintenance costs upon the launcher itself. Like its smaller counterpart, the Light Torpedo missile, the Triton is extremely resilient to countermeasures.
missile_torpedo_heavy_mk1.name: Heavy Torpedo Missile Mk1

missile_torpedo_light_mk1.description: Torpedoes are significantly slower than other missile weapons but they provide far and away more firepower on impact than any other type. They are best utilised, therefore, against less agile ships and emplacements that cannot easily avoid the impact simply by moving out of the way. The Light Torpedo missile is a hot-launched weapon. This means the rocket motor fires immediately upon button-press and begins its slow, ponderous journey towards the target. This in turn imparts significantly more stress and maintenance costs upon the launcher itself. With an enlarged launcher comes a larger body which allows significantly more room for explosive mass in the weapon. This torpedo is a two-stage weapon with a powerful high explosive precursor warhead that violently rips a hole in the enemy vessel. A primary charge then detonates and turns the weapon into a massive and extremely damaging fragmentation grenade, more than powerful enough to rip apart most ships in a single blow. The Light Torpedo missile is extremely resilient to countermeasures.
missile_torpedo_light_mk1.name: Light Torpedo Missile Mk1

missilecomponents.description: Missile components are used in the construction of all missiles, often with a different number and configuration making up the construction of each missile. As with ship equipment, missiles are built on demand, and so missile components are bought at and found at shipyards and equipment docks, where most pilots can order missiles on demand.
missilecomponents.name: Missile Components

mod_engine_travelattacktime_01_mk1.name: Spur (Travel Attack Time Mod - Basic Quality)

mod_engine_travelchargetime_01_mk1.name: Overdrive (Travel Charge Time Mod - Basic Quality)

mod_engine_travelreleasetime_01_mk1.name: Anchor (Travel Release Time Mod - Basic Quality)

mod_engine_travelstartthrust_01_mk1.name: Afterburner (Travel Start Thrust Mod - Basic Quality)

mod_ship_hidecargo_01.name: Mirage (Illegal Ware Concealment Mod - Exceptional Quality)

mod_ship_regiondamage_01_mk1.name: Grit (Hazardous Region Damage Reduction Mod - Basic Quality)

mod_ship_regiondamage_01_mk2.name: Mettle (Hazardous Region Damage Reduction Mod - Enhanced Quality)

mod_ship_regiondamage_01_mk3.name: Tenacity (Hazardous Region Damage Reduction Mod - Exceptional Quality)

mod_weapon_sticktime_01_mk1.name: Gum (Sticky Projectile Time Mod - Basic Quality)

mod_weapon_sticktime_01_mk2.name: Cement (Sticky Projectile Time Mod - Enhanced Quality)

mod_weapon_sticktime_02_mk2.name: Glue (Sticky Projectile Time Mod - Enhanced Quality)

mod_weapon_sticktime_03_mk2.name: Adhesive (Sticky Projectile Time Mod - Exceptional Quality)
