mod_weapon_surfaceelement_01_mk3.name: Invader (Surface Element Damage Mod - Exceptional Quality)

modpart_enginefuelinjector_t1.description: Though ship engines no longer require liquid fuel, if it can be safely added to the mixture that powers the engine, it may well be possible to improve engine properties. This requires a fuel injector, not used in ship engines since liquid fuel was a requirement. As they have now fallen out of use, many pilots have simply thrown them away or scrapped them for parts, and so they may be difficult to come across.
modpart_enginefuelinjector_t1.name: Basic Engine Fuel Injector

modpart_enginefuelinjector_t2.description: Though ship engines no longer require liquid fuel, if it can be safely added to the mixture that powers the engine, it may well be possible to improve engine properties. This requires a fuel injector, not used in ship engines since liquid fuel was a requirement. As they have now fallen out of use, many pilots have simply thrown them away or scrapped them for parts, and so they may be difficult to come across.
modpart_enginefuelinjector_t2.name: Advanced Engine Fuel Injector

modpart_enginefuelinjector_t3.description: Though ship engines no longer require liquid fuel, if it can be safely added to the mixture that powers the engine, it may well be possible to improve engine properties. This requires a fuel injector, not used in ship engines since liquid fuel was a requirement. As they have now fallen out of use, many pilots have simply thrown them away or scrapped them for parts, and so they may be difficult to come across.
modpart_enginefuelinjector_t3.name: Exceptional Engine Fuel Injector

modpart_extendedfuelcontainer.description: Though it has now been a while since ship engines required liquid fuel to function, these fuel containers can still be found throughout the Jump Gate network, often used to contain something completely different, and sometimes used by modders in engine modifications.
modpart_extendedfuelcontainer.name: Extended Fuel Container

modpart_highenergycatalyst.description: Whether it be a rock or metal, or something man-made, these objects when exposed to specific elements will cause a short but strong and surprising explosion that can be used for a variety of purposes.
modpart_highenergycatalyst.name: High Energy Catalyst

modpart_nividiumcrystallite.description: Though a metal in its natural form, under the right conditions, nividium can become a light and strong crystalline structure. Though there are not many uses for this compound, it is rumoured that, among other things, some ship pilots use it and rare ship nanoweaves to improve properties of their ship hulls.
modpart_nividiumcrystallite.name: Nividium Crystallite

modpart_nividiumoxide.description: Nividium oxide is an unstable mixture that when mistreated can be both deadly and messy. When treated properly, however, and handled with care, it becomes a sure-fire, controlled way to produce extra energy in the form of force and excess heat. Racers across the Jump Gate network like to refer to the chemical as 'nox'.
modpart_nividiumoxide.name: Nividium Oxide

modpart_shieldgeneratorcoil_t1.description: As with all equipment attached to ships in the modern age, ship shields are built on-demand. This means that all shield components are shipped together directly to the shipyards and equipment docks that build and repair ships, and as most pilots are satisfied with what they are given, it is very uncommon to find individual components on their own. If, however, one can find a shield generator coil, it may be possible to modify an existing shield in such a way that the properties can be improved.
modpart_shieldgeneratorcoil_t1.name: Basic Shield Generator Coil

modpart_shieldgeneratorcoil_t2.description: As with all equipment attached to ships in the modern age, ship shields are built on-demand. This means that all shield components are shipped together directly to the shipyards and equipment docks that build and repair ships, and as most pilots are satisfied with what they are given, it is very uncommon to find individual components on their own. If, however, one can find a shield generator coil, it may be possible to modify an existing shield in such a way that the properties can be improved.
modpart_shieldgeneratorcoil_t2.name: Advanced Shield Generator Coil

modpart_shieldgeneratorcoil_t3.description: As with all equipment attached to ships in the modern age, ship shields are built on-demand. This means that all shield components are shipped together directly to the shipyards and equipment docks that build and repair ships, and as most pilots are satisfied with what they are given, it is very uncommon to find individual components on their own. If, however, one can find a shield generator coil, it may be possible to modify an existing shield in such a way that the properties can be improved.
modpart_shieldgeneratorcoil_t3.name: Exceptional Shield Generator Coil

modpart_shipnanoweave_t1.description: Though today, shipyards across the Jump Gate network are trusted to construct ship hulls from blueprints using hull parts constructed from metals or metal-equivalents, it is rumoured that during the Terran conflict, Beryll developed nanoweaves capable of mimicking or adjusting ship hulls in very specific ways. If these nanoweaves can be found, and understood, it may well be possible to use them to tweak properties of existing ships to make improvements.
modpart_shipnanoweave_t1.name: Basic Ship Nanoweave

modpart_shipnanoweave_t2.description: Though today, shipyards across the Jump Gate network are trusted to construct ship hulls from blueprints using hull parts constructed from metals or metal-equivalents, it is rumoured that during the Terran conflict, Beryll developed nanoweaves capable of mimicking or adjusting ship hulls in very specific ways. If these nanoweaves can be found, and understood, it may well be possible to use them to tweak properties of existing ships to make improvements.
modpart_shipnanoweave_t2.name: Advanced Ship Nanoweave

modpart_shipnanoweave_t3.description: Though today, shipyards across the Jump Gate network are trusted to construct ship hulls from blueprints using hull parts constructed from metals or metal-equivalents, it is rumoured that during the Terran conflict, Beryll developed nanoweaves capable of mimicking or adjusting ship hulls in very specific ways. If these nanoweaves can be found, and understood, it may well be possible to use them to tweak properties of existing ships to make improvements.
modpart_shipnanoweave_t3.name: Exceptional Ship Nanoweave

modpart_tuningsoftware.description: While modifying hardware can take you very far improving a variety of equipment, tuning software can also be used to make improvements to the equipment's algorithms and calculations. This may unlock extra potential in the hardware; overriding limits placed on the systems by manufacturers, or simply optimising existing software in such a way that equipment can be used for efficiently.
modpart_tuningsoftware.name: Tuning Software

modpart_weaponchamber_t1.description: Though not all weapons, in fact very few, use projectile bullets in the modern day, most still have the equivalent to a chamber, where the shot that will be fired is either loaded or prepared. It is then from the chamber that the bullet is fired. As weapons are now built on-demand, and very few pilots have reason to dismantle them, it is uncommon to find detached weapon chamber. However, if one can be found, it may be possible to modify an existing weapon in a way hitherto unthought of.
modpart_weaponchamber_t1.name: Basic Weapon Chamber

modpart_weaponchamber_t2.description: Though not all weapons, in fact very few, use projectile bullets in the modern day, most still have the equivalent to a chamber, where the shot that will be fired is either loaded or prepared. It is then from the chamber that the bullet is fired. As weapons are now built on-demand, and very few pilots have reason to dismantle them, it is uncommon to find detached weapon chamber. However, if one can be found, it may be possible to modify an existing weapon in a way hitherto unthought of.
modpart_weaponchamber_t2.name: Advanced Weapon Chamber

modpart_weaponchamber_t3.description: Though not all weapons, in fact very few, use projectile bullets in the modern day, most still have the equivalent to a chamber, where the shot that will be fired is either loaded or prepared. It is then from the chamber that the bullet is fired. As weapons are now built on-demand, and very few pilots have reason to dismantle them, it is uncommon to find detached weapon chamber. However, if one can be found, it may be possible to modify an existing weapon in a way hitherto unthought of.
modpart_weaponchamber_t3.name: Exceptional Weapon Chamber

module_gen_dock_m_showroom_01.description: Let's face it, owning a spaceship is a necessity in almost all civilised regions of space, no matter how densely populated the official census data makes them appear. Owning two spaceships differentiates the entrepreneur from the middle class, private owner. However, owning three, six, twelve - or however many - spaceships elevates the distinguished gentle-being to the upper class, the one-percenters that can. And if we're honest, what multi-millio-, billio-, quadrillionaire would place their shiny valuable spacecrafts into a parking orbit around some inconsequential moon in a godforsaken star system, never to be appreciated by anyone? That is right, none!nnThe rule of wealth goes like this: if you own it, show it. Place it into a lustrous hall, have its hull polished by an army of exquisite robot hands, bathed in blinding lights by a laser beam from above. And then, if you have your romantic interest over for dinner, or your prospective business partner - or your grand elder-being, for that matter - parade them past your luxury vehicles.nnYou earned it!
module_gen_dock_m_showroom_01.name: Small & Medium Ship Showroom

module_gen_proc_scrapworks.description: The Jump Gate shutdown left many sectors with very limited access to resources, prompting stranded Argon engineers to begin development of advanced recycling facilities. One of these was the Scrap Processor; a module designed to process S and M-sized ship wrecks into scrap, which in turn can be recycled into other useable resources. The first Tug ships were also introduced at this time, to transport the wrecks to the Scrap Processor. Scrap Cubes were a later introduction, designed to allow larger wrecks to be compacted into blocks suitable for towing and processing.nnThe arrival of the first interstellar messenger drones after the Fall of the Gates encouraged the administrations of many sectors to share survival-critical information with now inaccessible neighbouring systems, including blueprints for the new recycling technology. The aim was to help resource-disadvantaged populations, in the hope of receiving important information in return.nnThis system of mutual information exchange worked so well that many sectors maintained it locally even after Jump Gate connections were restored.
module_gen_proc_scrapworks.name: Scrap Processor

module_gen_proc_scrapworkskhaak_01.description: The Jump Gate shutdown left many sectors with very limited access to resources, prompting stranded engineers to design advanced recycling facilities. One of these was the Allographyne Scrap Processor; a module designed to process Kha'ak ship wrecks into Allographyne Fragments, which in turn can be recycled into other useable resources.nnThe arrival of the first interstellar messenger drones after the Fall of the Gates encouraged the administrations of many sectors to share survival-critical information with now inaccessible neighbouring systems, including blueprints for the new recycling technology. The aim was to support resource-disadvantaged populations, in the hope of receiving important information in return.nnThis system of mutual information exchange worked so well that many sectors maintained it locally even after Jump Gate connections were restored.
module_gen_proc_scrapworkskhaak_01.name: Allographyne Scrap Processor

module_gen_prod_scrap_recycler.description: The Jump Gate shutdown left many sectors with very limited access to resources, prompting stranded Argon engineers to begin development of advanced recycling facilities. One of these was the Scrap Recycler; a module designed to process scrap into usable resources, converting them into Hull Parts and Claytronics in alternating production cycles.nnThe arrival of the first interstellar messenger drones after the Fall of the Gates encouraged the administrations of many sectors to share survival-critical information with now inaccessible neighbouring systems, including blueprints for the new recycling technology. The aim was to help resource-disadvantaged populations, in the hope of receiving important information in return.nnThis system of mutual information exchange worked so well that many sectors maintained it locally even after Jump Gate connections were restored.
module_gen_prod_scrap_recycler.name: Scrap Recycler

module_gen_prod_scrap_recyclerkhaak.description: The Jump Gate shutdown left many sectors with very limited access to resources, prompting stranded engineers to design advanced recycling facilities. One of these was the Allographyne Scrap Recycler; a module designed to recycle Allographyne Fragments into usable resources by converting them into Allographyne.nnThe arrival of the first interstellar messenger drones after the Fall of the Gates encouraged the administrations of many sectors to share survival-critical information with now inaccessible neighbouring systems, including blueprints for the new recycling technology. The aim was to support resource-disadvantaged populations, in the hope of receiving important information in return.nnThis system of mutual information exchange worked so well that many sectors maintained it locally even after Jump Gate connections were restored.
module_gen_prod_scrap_recyclerkhaak.name: Allographyne Scrap Recycler

module_gen_welfare_gamblinghall_01.description: A station offering a wide range of recreational activities can help set it apart from its competition, and will improve conditions for its workforce. Gambling establishments increase the rate at which a station's workforce grows.nnThe Gambling Den is a modest, but cosy, space where workers can gather to gamble away their savings after a long shift. The establishment offers tables with a wide variety of games, and a bar for people to take occasional breaks and have some refreshments.
module_gen_welfare_gamblinghall_01.name: Gambling Den

module_tel_tradestation_conn_bridge_01.description: As well as acting as secondary support for the Habitat-class superstation's primary ring, the bridge also provides high-speed transport from the ring to the complex centre and ring extension, allowing citizens and station workers to travel around the station.
module_tel_tradestation_conn_bridge_01.name: Habitat Bridge

module_tel_tradestation_conn_column_01.description: Used primarily for structural support between a Habitat-class superstation's centre and its primary ring, this spoke-like column also holds quarters for the station's workers and inhabitants.
module_tel_tradestation_conn_column_01.name: Habitat Ring Column

module_tel_tradestation_def_center_01.description: Forming the heart of the Habitat-class superstation, the HCC houses most of its administrative sectors, as well as about a third of its living space.
module_tel_tradestation_def_center_01.name: Habitat Centre Complex

module_tel_tradestation_def_extension_01.description: This module extends the Habitat-class superstation so that it can support additional modules behind its primary ring. These attach to the extension module in a spoke-like fashion.
module_tel_tradestation_def_extension_01.name: Habitat Ring Extension

module_tel_tradestation_def_ringcenter_0.description: Forming the central structural support to the Habitat-class superstation's primary ring, this small ring also extends from the Habitat Centre Complex to act as an entrance to the station for any ships that wish to dock.
module_tel_tradestation_def_ringcenter_0.name: Habitat Centre Ring

module_tel_tradestation_hab_inside_ring_.description: Placed in the inner-side of the Habitat-class superstation's primary ring, the city is home to thousands of citizens and station workers. When designing the station, the Teladi Company attempted to make the city suitable not just for Teladi, but for as many species as possible, and so it is not unusual to find cities with many different biomes.
module_tel_tradestation_hab_inside_ring_.name: Habitat City

module_tel_tradestation_hab_inside_ring_.description: Placed in the inner-side of the Habitat-class superstation's primary ring, the city is home to thousands of citizens and station workers. When designing the station, the Teladi Company attempted to make the city suitable not just for Teladi, but for as many species as possible, and so it is not unusual to find cities with many different biomes.
module_tel_tradestation_hab_inside_ring_.name: Habitat City

module_tel_tradestation_pier_tradelocati.description: These components of the Habitat-class superstation house huge trading floors where official and civilian traders can go to trade wares and produce.
module_tel_tradestation_pier_tradelocati.name: Habitat Trade Centre

nividium.description: A rare metal found in small pockets in asteroids, Nividium has been cut out of any economic use by the improved efficiency with which businesses have been able to use ore and silicon, much easier to find and cheaper to process. However, Nividium is still valuable, and is often transported planet-side to be made into jewelry and art.
nividium.name: Nividium

nostropoil.description: Nostrop oil is squeezed from the leaves of sunrise flowers and mixed with water. Though the Teladi favour it for its simplicity and nutrients, other species have clearly indicated that nostrop oil does not in fact taste at all pleasing.
nostropoil.name: Nostrop Oil

ore.description: Today ore tends not to be mined on habitable worlds, but harvested from other celestial bodies, mainly asteroids. As could be expected, Ore must always be refined to be of any use.
ore.name: Ore

plasmaconductors.description: These conductors are designed to allow the flow of plasma through a component at the greatest possible efficiency. Though made relatively cheap to produce through years of research and development, plasma conductors remain highly complex pieces of technology. They are used primarily in the construction of weapon and shield components.
plasmaconductors.name: Plasma Conductors

quantumtubes.description: An example of complex technology being made cheap through years of continuous research, no one outside of those manufacturing the technology quite knows how quantum tubes work. Though the outer wiring itself it easy to make and understand, the internal structure and what the wiring actually carries must be constructed only by those with countless hours of study and examination under their belts; otherwise risking catastrophic failures that can lead to entire shutdowns of the equipment in which they are used.
quantumtubes.name: Quantum Tubes

rawkhaakscrap.description: While Allographyne is rarely manufactured in the Community of Planets, Kha'ak wreckage typically contains salvageable amounts of Allographyne Scrap that can be reprocessed into Allographyne Fragments. In a further step, these Fragments can then be integrated into a wide array of established manufacturing workflows.
rawkhaakscrap.name: Allographyne Scrap

rawscrap.description: Raw Scrap is found in wrecks. It is extracted from the wrecks of capital ships and stations, and broken down into Scrap Cubes by Compactor ships. These Scrap Cubes, as well as whole smaller wrecks, can be collected by Tug ships and taken to Scrap Processors, where they are melted down into Scrap Metal for further recycling.
rawscrap.name: Raw Scrap

refinedmetals.description: Refined from ore found in countless asteroids across the Jump Gate network, these refined metals are cheap to produce and easy to reinforce, making them perfect for use in constructing all kinds of Hull Parts, not just for ships and stations, but also for smaller components that used across all of space.
refinedmetals.name: Refined Metals

research_agentslot_01.description: Primary Embassy upgrade enabling management and deployment of additional Agents.
research_agentslot_01.name: Embassy Management - Upgrade I

research_agentslot_02.description: Secondary Embassy upgrade enabling management and deployment of the maximum number of Agents.
research_agentslot_02.name: Embassy Management - Upgrade II

research_diplomacy_network.description: Grants access to the Embassy at Headquarters, enabling diplomatic opportunities.
research_diplomacy_network.name: Diplomacy Network

research_equipment_xenon.description: This research will unlock the ability to modify the mysterious new variants of Xenon ships at personal ship production installations.
research_equipment_xenon.name: Xenon Special Equipment

research_high_mass_teleportation.description: Seemingly based on technology used to tunnel to parallel universes, this function of the station is capable of moving massive structures to another pre-determined location.
research_high_mass_teleportation.name: High-mass Teleportation

research_interference_network.description: Enables the deployment of autonomous intelligence cells operating under Protocol Null. These deep-cover units conduct unsupervised operations designed to provoke diplomatic friction between factions, independent of direct oversight.
research_interference_network.name: Undercover Interference Network

research_mod_engine_mk1.description: Allows the crafting of basic engine mods for ships.
research_mod_engine_mk1.name: Basic Engine Mods

research_mod_engine_mk2.description: Allows the crafting of more advanced engine mods for ships.
research_mod_engine_mk2.name: Advanced Engine Mods

research_mod_engine_mk3.description: Allows the crafting of superior engine mods for ships.
research_mod_engine_mk3.name: Exceptional Engine Mods

research_mod_engine_pre.description: High-grade sensors are required for development of engine technology.
research_mod_engine_pre.name: Acquire Engine Sensors

research_mod_shield_mk1.description: Allows the crafting of basic shield mods for ships.
research_mod_shield_mk1.name: Basic Shield Mods

research_mod_shield_mk2.description: Allows the crafting of more advanced shield mods for ships.
research_mod_shield_mk2.name: Advanced Shield Mods

research_mod_shield_mk3.description: Allows the crafting of superior shield mods for ships.
research_mod_shield_mk3.name: Exceptional Shield Mods

research_mod_shield_pre.description: Live combat data will help in developing more advanced shield technology.
research_mod_shield_pre.name: Acquire Weapon Damage Data

research_mod_ship_mk1.description: Allows the crafting of basic chassis mods for ships.
research_mod_ship_mk1.name: Basic Chassis Mods

research_mod_ship_mk2.description: Allows the crafting of more advanced chassis mods for ships.
research_mod_ship_mk2.name: Advanced Chassis Mods

research_mod_ship_mk3.description: Allows the crafting of superior chassis mods for ships.
research_mod_ship_mk3.name: Exceptional Chassis Mods

research_mod_ship_pre.description: Nanites will allow us to easily modify a ship chassis and bring out its potential.
research_mod_ship_pre.name: Acquire Nanites

research_mod_weapon_mk1.description: Allows the crafting of basic weapon mods for ships.
research_mod_weapon_mk1.name: Basic Weapon Mods

research_mod_weapon_mk2.description: Allows the crafting of more advanced weapon mods for ships.
research_mod_weapon_mk2.name: Advanced Weapon Mods

research_mod_weapon_mk3.description: Allows the crafting of superior weapon mods for ships.
research_mod_weapon_mk3.name: Exceptional Weapon Mods

research_mod_weapon_pre.description: To improve the chances of creating safe and effective weapon modifications, high-spec particle regulators are required.
research_mod_weapon_pre.name: Acquire Regulators

research_module_build.description: Allows the possibility to steal build module blueprints when scanning their data leaks.
research_module_build.name: Build Module Hack

research_module_defence.description: Allows the possibility to steal defence module blueprints when scanning their data leaks.
research_module_defence.name: Defence Module Hack

research_module_dock.description: Allows the possibility to steal Dock Module blueprints when scanning their data leaks.
research_module_dock.name: Dock Module Hack

research_module_habitation.description: Allows the possibility to steal habitation module blueprints when scanning their data leaks.
research_module_habitation.name: Habitation Module Hack

research_module_production.description: Allows the possibility to steal production module blueprints when scanning their data leaks.
research_module_production.name: Production Module Hack

research_module_storage.description: Allows the possibility to steal storage module blueprints when scanning their data leaks.
research_module_storage.name: Storage Module Hack

research_module_venture.description: Allows the construction of experimental modules to send ships to parallel universes. (Requires you to be signed in to online features.)
research_module_venture.name: Venture Docks

research_module_welfare_1.description: A certain nearby station is enticing workers from far and wide. What makes it so alluring, and can we achieve the same effect?
research_module_welfare_1.name: Welfare Module - Gambling Den

research_radioreceiver.description: Allows you to receive radio transmissions from throughout space.
research_radioreceiver.name: Radio Receiver

research_sensorbooster.description: Allows you to scan station modules for more information.
research_sensorbooster.name: Sensor Booster

research_seta.description: Unlocks construction of the Singularity Engine Time Accelerator (SETA), a device that locally compresses perceived time. Primarily intended to reduce transit duration during extended travel phases.
research_seta.name: Time Compression

research_seta_pre.description: This initiative serves as a prerequisite for enabling Boso Ta to commence research into time-compression technology.
research_seta_pre.name: Time Compression Prerequisite

research_teleportation.description: A by-product of Terran warp research, this will allow teleportation of an individual over a limited range.
research_teleportation.name: Teleportation

research_teleportation_range_01.description: Increases the range of the personal teleporter from within 1 system to within 3.
research_teleportation_range_01.name: Teleportation - Range I

research_teleportation_range_02.description: Increases the range of the personal teleporter from within 3 systems to within 5.
research_teleportation_range_02.name: Teleportation - Range II

research_teleportation_range_03.description: Increases the range of the personal teleporter so that you can jump to ships you own across the entire Jump Gate network.
research_teleportation_range_03.name: Teleportation - Range III

research_tradeinterface.description: Allows you to access trade information from around stations.
research_tradeinterface.name: Trade Interface

research_warp_hq_01.description: Replicating the conditions often observed near spatial anomalies, enact an experiment to relocate this facility within its current sector.
research_warp_hq_01.name: High-mass Teleportation I

research_warp_hq_02.description: Allows the facility to be relocated to a marked location within any known sector.
research_warp_hq_02.name: High-mass Teleportation II

research_xenon_crisis_01.description: 033#text_error#Warning: May lead to an overwhelming crisis.033XnnTaking advantage of the extensive presence of your ships throughout the Jump Gate network, this research will pool and analyse vast amounts of data in the hope of unravelling the mysteries of the universe.
research_xenon_crisis_01.name: Fleet-Wide Sensor Analytics

research_xenon_crisis_02.description: The data from the previous Xenon-Kha'ak crisis has been exhausted of all reasonable usefulness. To gain more insight into such Xenon behaviour an attack signal could be encoded with the CPU ship's signature to trigger another phase of aggression. Whether the actual CPU ship halts or commits to these new assaults is unknown.nn033#text_error#Warning: Research may lead to; criminal charges, extinction, incarceration, heightened blood-pressure (applicable species only), injury and loss of property.033X
research_xenon_crisis_02.name: Xenon Intelligence Study

resourceprobe_01.description: Often fired from scout and mining ships deep within a mineral or gas region, resource probes are able to scan both asteroids and gas clouds in order to analyse what elements make up the region, in order to give miners a better idea of what they can find there. Not only can a resource probe discover the different elements making up the field, but also give a rough estimate on its yield.
resourceprobe_01.name: Resource Probe

satellite_mk1.description: A static object used for observation, once deployed a satellite will pick up any communications sent and received by both ships and stations on open frequencies within a certain range. They are not one-use objects; a satellite can be deployed in one location, then deactivated and picked back up in a ship, then redeployed in a new location.
satellite_mk1.name: Satellite

satellite_mk2.description: A static object used for observation, once deployed a satellite will pick up any communications sent and received by both ships and stations on open frequencies within a certain range. They are not one-use objects; a satellite can be deployed in one location, then deactivated and picked back up in a ship, then redeployed in a new location.
satellite_mk2.name: Advanced Satellite

scanningarrays.description: Scanning arrays have seen elegant redesign over the years so that ships and stations do not risk fragile antennas being broken or destroyed by collisions. Now entirely internal systems, scanning arrays are used not just in ship and station scanners and radars, but are also used in the targeting systems supplied with turret and drone components.
scanningarrays.name: Scanning Arrays

scrapmetal.description: Scrap Metal has become established as a vital part of severely resource-deprived economies. Usually obtained from salvaged station segments or recovered ship wrecks, Scrap Metal can be recycled into other, more valuable, materials.
scrapmetal.name: Scrap Metal

shieldcomponents.description: From generators to coils that maintain energy over time, shield components are a wide arrange of technologies that provide everything necessary to build a variety of shields. Completely modular, each part can be used in each shield, which has led to a wide variety of new-generation shields being produced in recent years. As with all ship equipment, shields are built on-demand, and so shield components are produced in factories and shipped straight to shipyards and equipment docks where they can be more freely used for construction and repair.
shieldcomponents.name: Shield Components

ship_arg_l_destroyer_01_a.description: The original design of the Behemoth-class destroyer was put into development mid-way through the Kha'ak conflict, during which the Titan-class was beginning to show its age and inefficiencies. At first too expensive for a crippled Argon economy, then too complex to produce quickly during the Terran Conflict, the first Behemoth did not ship out until long after that war had ended, and the Jump Gates had begun to catastrophically fail. It was only when Argon Prime and Black Hole Sun came back into contact that the Behemoth could be rolled out more frequently.
ship_arg_l_destroyer_01_a.name: Behemoth Vanguard

ship_arg_l_destroyer_01_b.description: The original design of the Behemoth-class destroyer was put into development mid-way through the Kha'ak conflict, during which the Titan-class was beginning to show its age and inefficiencies. At first too expensive for a crippled Argon economy, then too complex to produce quickly during the Terran Conflict, the first Behemoth did not ship out until long after that war had ended, and the Jump Gates had begun to catastrophically fail. It was only when Argon Prime and Black Hole Sun came back into contact that the Behemoth could be rolled out more frequently.
ship_arg_l_destroyer_01_b.name: Behemoth Sentinel

ship_arg_l_destroyer_02_a.description: The original design of the Behemoth-class destroyer was put into development mid-way through the Kha'ak conflict, during which the Titan-class was beginning to show its age and inefficiencies. ship_arg_l_destroyer_02_a.name: Behemoth E

ship_arg_l_miner_liquid_01_a.description: As the trend towards collecting natural resources with ships continued, the inefficiencies of the solution started to become clear. While a medium-sized ship had the advantage of moving quickly through mineral and gas regions, their cargo bays were simply not big enough for industrial-scale mining to be lucrative.nnNaturally, the Teladi stepped in to design L-sized resource collectors that could solve that problem, and the Magnetar is the Argon response to these ships; a standardised model that can be built in two variants, allowing it to support both asteroid mining and natural gas collection.
ship_arg_l_miner_liquid_01_a.name: Magnetar (Gas) Vanguard

ship_arg_l_trans_container_01_a.description: Designed and produced by the Antigone Republic after the Jump Gates began to realign, the Veles-class freighter is more compact and self-sufficient than any freighter-class ship before it; the reason why it became part of the on-going technology exchange between the Republic and their Argon Federation allies.nnBuilt using a highly modular design, many ship designers have taken the base Veles model and reorganised it, as well as making their own changes to the internal systems, to sell an ever-growing number of variations on the ship. However, the Veles remains a popular choice among traders and station builders.
ship_arg_l_trans_container_01_a.name: Veles Vanguard

ship_arg_l_trans_container_01_b.description: Designed and produced by the Antigone Republic after the Jump Gates began to realign, the Veles-class freighter is more compact and self-sufficient than any freighter-class ship before it; the reason why it became part of the on-going technology exchange between the Republic and their Argon Federation allies.nnBuilt using a highly modular design, many ship designers have taken the base Veles model and reorganised it, as well as making their own changes to the internal systems, to sell an ever-growing number of variations on the ship. However, the Veles remains a popular choice among traders and station builders.
ship_arg_l_trans_container_01_b.name: Veles Sentinel

ship_arg_l_trans_container_02_a.description: When Argon Federation ship designers received the blue-prints for the Veles, it was immediately recognisable as a highly modular ship, and so they began work straight away on different variations that could provide more variety and competition in a ship class that was both needed and popular throughout Argon space.nnOne of the first ships to come from this process was the Mokosi, designed in Black Hole Sun. Though it uses the same base chassis as the Veles, a different container configuration and tweaks to several internal systems to adapt have created a fine addition to the Argon merchant fleet.
ship_arg_l_trans_container_02_a.name: Mokosi Vanguard

ship_arg_l_trans_container_02_b.description: When Argon Federation ship designers received the blue-prints for the Veles, it was immediately recognisable as a highly modular ship, and so they began work straight away on different variations that could provide more variety and competition in a ship class that was both needed and popular throughout Argon space.nnOne of the first ships to come from this process was the Mokosi, designed in Black Hole Sun. Though it uses the same base chassis as the Veles, a different container configuration and tweaks to several internal systems to adapt have created a fine addition to the Argon merchant fleet.
ship_arg_l_trans_container_02_b.name: Mokosi Sentinel

ship_arg_l_trans_container_03_a.description: Designed and produced as part of the push to revitalise the Argon economy after the Jump Gate realignment, the relatively new Incarcatura has become a popular alternative to the Veles and Mokosi design. Though there have been some concerns as to its length, that it might caught flight problems, most experts are willing to overlook these problems due to its cargo efficiency.
ship_arg_l_trans_container_03_a.name: Incarcatura Vanguard

ship_arg_l_trans_container_03_b.description: Designed and produced as part of the push to revitalise the Argon economy after the Jump Gate realignment, the relatively new Incarcatura has become a popular alternative to the Veles and Mokosi design. Though there have been some concerns as to its length, that it might caught flight problems, most experts are willing to overlook these problems due to its cargo efficiency.
ship_arg_l_trans_container_03_b.name: Incarcatura Sentinel

ship_arg_l_trans_container_04_a.description: Designed in parallel to the Incarcatura, as part of the push to rejuvenate the Argon economy, the Shuyaku-class freighter is a Sonra variant, extending the Terracorp-designed freighter's cargo bay at the cost of some manoeuvrability. The Shuyaku also marks the Hatikvah Free League's first contribution to the Argon merchant fleet, meant as an offering of good will to both the Federation and the Republic, to show that the Free League was also taking the rebuilding effort seriously.
ship_arg_l_trans_container_04_a.name: Shuyaku Vanguard

ship_arg_l_trans_container_04_b.description: Designed in parallel to the Incarcatura, as part of the push to rejuvenate the Argon economy, the Shuyaku-class freighter is a Sonra variant, extending the Terracorp-designed freighter's cargo bay at the cost of some manoeuvrability. The Shuyaku also marks the Hatikvah Free League's first contribution to the Argon merchant fleet, meant as an offering of good will to both the Federation and the Republic, to show that the Free League was also taking the rebuilding effort seriously.
ship_arg_l_trans_container_04_b.name: Shuyaku Sentinel

ship_arg_l_trans_container_05_a.description: Though the Sonra-class freighter was initially designed by Terracorp after the Terran Conflict, the status of development was lost with contact to Home of Light. Quite by chance, project designers were trapped in Black Hole Sun during the Jump Gate shutdown, and were able to get the blueprints of the finished ship to the Argon Federation and Antigone Republic when contact with Argon Prime and Antigone Memorial was re-established.
ship_arg_l_trans_container_05_a.name: Sonra Vanguard

ship_arg_l_trans_container_05_b.description: Though the Sonra-class freighter was initially designed by Terracorp after the Terran Conflict, the status of development was lost with contact to Home of Light. Quite by chance, project designers were trapped in Black Hole Sun during the Jump Gate shutdown, and were able to get the blueprints of the finished ship to the Argon Federation and Antigone Republic when contact with Argon Prime and Antigone Memorial was re-established.
ship_arg_l_trans_container_05_b.name: Sonra Sentinel

ship_arg_m_bomber_01_a.description: A ship that saw much upheaval behind the scenes during the Terran Conflict, when it became painfully clear to the Argon that their ships were far inferior to the Terran equivalents, the Minotaur was over the course of the conflict completely redesigned as a mobile weapons-platform, sporting heavy weapons that deal incredible amounts of damage to similarly-sized and larger, slower targets. Unfortunately, the ship was unable to be put into mass-production before crisis crippled the Argon Federation, and as a result the new Minotaur has only recently entered service.
ship_arg_m_bomber_01_a.name: Minotaur Vanguard

ship_arg_m_bomber_01_b.description: A ship that saw much upheaval behind the scenes during the Terran Conflict, when it became painfully clear to the Argon that their ships were far inferior to the Terran equivalents, the Minotaur was over the course of the conflict completely redesigned as a mobile weapons-platform, sporting heavy weapons that deal incredible amounts of damage to similarly-sized and larger, slower targets. Unfortunately, the ship was unable to be put into mass-production before crisis crippled the Argon Federation, and as a result the new Minotaur has only recently entered service.
ship_arg_m_bomber_01_b.name: Minotaur Sentinel

ship_arg_m_bomber_02_a.description: A ship that saw much upheaval behind the scenes during the Terran Conflict, when it became painfully clear to the Argon that their ships were far inferior to the Terran equivalents, the Minotaur was over the course of the conflict completely redesigned as a mobile weapons-platform, sporting heavy weapons that deal incredible amounts of damage to similarly-sized and larger, slower targets. Unfortunately, the ship was unable to be put into mass-production before crisis crippled the Argon Federation, and as a result the new Minotaur has only recently entered service.
ship_arg_m_bomber_02_a.name: Minotaur Raider

ship_arg_m_frigate_01_a.description: The first of the newly-designed M-sized support ships to reach the Federal and Republic navies, the Cerberus-class frigate is primarily designed to target and support smaller and similarly-sized vessels in a defensive capacity. Notably, the Cerberus was also the first ship of its size to support a fully-functional dock for smaller ships; a massive technical challenge for its designers. This has only made it a more popular choice of ship, and saw a new design race explode throughout the Jump Gate network as other ship designers scrambled to copy the Argons' innovative design.
ship_arg_m_frigate_01_a.name: Cerberus Vanguard

ship_arg_m_frigate_01_b.description: The first of the newly-designed M-sized support ships to reach the Federal and Republic navies, the Cerberus-class frigate is primarily designed to target and support smaller and similarly-sized vessels in a defensive capacity. Notably, the Cerberus was also the first ship of its size to support a fully-functional dock for smaller ships; a massive technical challenge for its designers. This has only made it a more popular choice of ship, and saw a new design race explode throughout the Jump Gate network as other ship designers scrambled to copy the Argons' innovative design.
ship_arg_m_frigate_01_b.name: Cerberus Sentinel

ship_arg_m_miner_liquid_01_a.description: Jonferco's response to the PMC's Drill design, the Sunder fulfils a similar role to its sister ship but handles gas collection instead of asteroid mining. It is fitted with a non-modular scoop that allows natural gases to be safely placed inside the ship's cargo hold. For all the gratitude received by PMC for their work in advancing asteroid mining, Jonferco's reply sent the mining community into a complete frenzy, with the possibilities for collecting, refining and utilising natural gases spurring the economy, not just in Argon space but across the entire Jump Gate network, to a brand new high.
ship_arg_m_miner_liquid_01_a.name: Sunder (Gas) Vanguard

ship_arg_m_miner_liquid_01_b.description: Jonferco's response to the PMC's Drill design, the Sunder fulfils a similar role to its sister ship but handles gas collection instead of asteroid mining. It is fitted with a non-modular scoop that allows natural gases to be safely placed inside the ship's cargo hold. For all the gratitude received by PMC for their work in advancing asteroid mining, Jonferco's reply sent the mining community into a complete frenzy, with the possibilities for collecting, refining and utilising natural gases spurring the economy, not just in Argon space but across the entire Jump Gate network, to a brand new high.
ship_arg_m_miner_liquid_01_b.name: Sunder (Gas) Sentinel

ship_arg_m_miner_solid_01_a.description: Towards the end of the last era, the Plutarch Mining Corporation called for a shift away from static station-based asteroid mining towards the far more efficient method of using ships to identify and break down asteroids and bring them to refineries in far less time than it took for a refinery to be attached to a much bigger asteroid. Though it is now different from the PMC's original design, the Drill has been in service ever since, inspiring a train of different mining ships to be released all across the old and new Jump Gate network.
ship_arg_m_miner_solid_01_a.name: Drill (Mineral) Vanguard

ship_arg_m_miner_solid_01_b.description: Towards the end of the last era, the Plutarch Mining Corporation called for a shift away from static station-based asteroid mining towards the far more efficient method of using ships to identify and break down asteroids and bring them to refineries in far less time than it took for a refinery to be attached to a much bigger asteroid. Though it is now different from the PMC's original design, the Drill has been in service ever since, inspiring a train of different mining ships to be released all across the old and new Jump Gate network.
ship_arg_m_miner_solid_01_b.name: Drill (Mineral) Sentinel

ship_arg_m_trans_container_01_a.description: The latest redesign of the Mercury-class transporter was almost purely aesthetic, with only small changes made to allow the user more modularity and modification options. Other than the aesthetic changes made to keep the model in line with latest style trends, the Mercury remains the same reliable M-sized transporter that has seen popular use across Argon space for so many years.
ship_arg_m_trans_container_01_a.name: Mercury Vanguard

ship_arg_m_trans_container_01_b.description: The latest redesign of the Mercury-class transporter was almost purely aesthetic, with only small changes made to allow the user more modularity and modification options. Other than the aesthetic changes made to keep the model in line with latest style trends, the Mercury remains the same reliable M-sized transporter that has seen popular use across Argon space for so many years.
ship_arg_m_trans_container_01_b.name: Mercury Sentinel

ship_arg_m_trans_container_02_a.description: With the Argon Federation desperate to rebuild its economy after the Jump Gates realigned, the sheer number of Mercury-class transporters across Argon space became so high that the value of the popular transporter began to sink rapidly. In an attempt to correct course, the Federation released the Ides. Though they are only slightly aesthetically and technically different, and in fact the Ides is based on the Mercury model, the ship has been pushed as competition to the popular transporter; a marketing strategy that has been well met by the public, and recently allowed for the ship market to find stability.
ship_arg_m_trans_container_02_a.name: Ides Vanguard

ship_arg_m_trans_container_02_b.description: With the Argon Federation desperate to rebuild its economy after the Jump Gates realigned, the sheer number of Mercury-class transporters across Argon space became so high that the value of the popular transporter began to sink rapidly. In an attempt to correct course, the Federation released the Ides. Though they are only slightly aesthetically and technically different, and in fact the Ides is based on the Mercury model, the ship has been pushed as competition to the popular transporter; a marketing strategy that has been well met by the public, and recently allowed for the ship market to find stability.
ship_arg_m_trans_container_02_b.name: Ides Sentinel

ship_arg_s_fighter_01_a.description: Though the prototype for the new-model Nova-class fighter was in development as far back as the Terran Conflict, it failed to reach production before the Jump Gate shutdown. Its first major role was escorting Argon Federation exploratory groups sent into newly discovered or rediscovered areas of space as the Jump Gates began to realign.nnThe new-look Nova is a major step up in ship design, with rotatable engines that make it formidable in strafing targets of a similar size. The Nova's redesign caused considerable controversy in the piloting community, when the rear turret was abandoned in favour of increased laser energy and strafing performance.nnDespite being a sound dogfighter, the Nova lacks the firepower required by full-time combatants. The Vanguard model improves the speed of the Nova. However, some pilots have suggested a portion of the upgrade budget might have been better spent on an aesthetic redesign.
ship_arg_s_fighter_01_a.name: Nova Vanguard

ship_arg_s_fighter_01_b.description: Though the prototype for the new-model Nova-class fighter was in development as far back as the Terran Conflict, it failed to reach production before the Jump Gate shutdown. Its first major role was escorting Argon Federation exploratory groups sent into newly discovered or rediscovered areas of space as the Jump Gates began to realign.nnThe new-look Nova is a major step up in ship design, with rotatable engines that make it formidable in strafing targets of a similar size. The Nova's redesign caused considerable controversy in the piloting community, when the rear turret was abandoned in favour of increased laser energy and strafing performance.nnDespite being a sound dogfighter, the Nova lacks the firepower required by full-time combatants. The Nova's Sentinel variant has a hardened hull, adding resilience, though this comes at a marginal cost to speed.
ship_arg_s_fighter_01_b.name: Nova Sentinel

ship_arg_s_fighter_02_a.description: Though retired from service for a long time before the Jump Gate shutdown, the push to give any advantage to the Federal and Republic fleets in their fights against the Xenon and the Holy Order of the Pontifex has seen the long-serving Elite redesigned as an interceptor.nnQuick in a straight line and able to fire off short bursts of fire-power before disengaging, the Elite often targets small ships and slower missiles, as well as using its speed and size, in support of the dog-fighters that are able to hold out longer under fire.
ship_arg_s_fighter_02_a.name: Elite Vanguard

ship_arg_s_fighter_02_b.description: Though retired from service for a long time before the Jump Gate shutdown, the push to give any advantage to the Federal and Republic fleets in their fights against the Xenon and the Holy Order of the Pontifex has seen the long-serving Elite redesigned as an interceptor.nnQuick in a straight line and able to fire off short bursts of fire-power before disengaging, the Elite often targets small ships and slower missiles, as well as using its speed and size, in support of the dog-fighters that are able to hold out longer under fire.
ship_arg_s_fighter_02_b.name: Elite Sentinel

ship_arg_s_fighter_03_a.description: When the Xenon destroyed a food production facility in Antigone Memorial, the sole survivor was protein engineer Tracie Harben. Having lost her entire family, and fuelled by vengeance, she dedicated herself to the creation of tools of destruction.nnHarben's most popular design is the Pulsar fighter, also known as the "Glass Cannon". A highly aggressive vessel, it prioritises armaments and speed above all else.nnNo-one knew the Pulsar could be even more aggressively optimised until Harben surprised observers by releasing the Vanguard model.
ship_arg_s_fighter_03_a.name: Pulsar Vanguard

ship_arg_s_fighter_04_a.description: In the midst of the Terran War, the Argon Federation floundered somewhat in their ship design output. Perhaps their worst effort was the Quasar fighter, which, named after a powerful cosmic force, failed to fully live up to expectations.nnThe Quasar Vanguard was commissioned to breathe some useful function into the model. However, the variant has failed to achieve popularity.
ship_arg_s_fighter_04_a.name: Quasar Vanguard

ship_arg_s_heavyfighter_01_a.description: Redesigned using the same combat-ready cockpit used by the new-look Nova, the Eclipse replaces the rotatable engines with a support structure that allows it to carry more weapons - effectively sacrificing the manoeuvrability of the dog-fighter for enough fire-power to be devastating against both small- and medium-sized ships.nnThis sacrifice does make the ship vulnerable to more specialised dog-fighters and interceptors, but nonetheless both the Federal and Republic navy has found the new Eclipse to be a worthy addition to their fleets, with Argon ship designers once again leading the race to design ships effective for both personal and military use.
ship_arg_s_heavyfighter_01_a.name: Eclipse Vanguard

ship_arg_s_heavyfighter_01_b.description: Redesigned using the same combat-ready cockpit used by the new-look Nova, the Eclipse replaces the rotatable engines with a support structure that allows it to carry more weapons - effectively sacrificing the manoeuvrability of the dog-fighter for enough fire-power to be devastating against both small- and medium-sized ships.nnThis sacrifice does make the ship vulnerable to more specialised dog-fighters and interceptors, but nonetheless both the Federal and Republic navy has found the new Eclipse to be a worthy addition to their fleets, with Argon ship designers once again leading the race to design ships effective for both personal and military use.
ship_arg_s_heavyfighter_01_b.name: Eclipse Sentinel

ship_arg_s_heavyfighter_02_a.description: Redesigned using the same combat-ready cockpit used by the new-look Nova, the Eclipse replaces the rotatable engines with a support structure that allows it to carry more weapons - effectively sacrificing the manoeuvrability of the dog-fighter for enough fire-power to be devastating against both small- and medium-sized ships.nnThis sacrifice does make the ship vulnerable to more specialised dog-fighters and interceptors, but nonetheless both the Federal and Republic navy has found the new Eclipse to be a worthy addition to their fleets, with Argon ship designers once again leading the race to design ships effective for both personal and military use.
ship_arg_s_heavyfighter_02_a.name: Eclipse Vanguard

ship_arg_s_miner_solid_01_a.description: After years of outcry from many a trader about the lack of a small and speedy transporter, the up and coming ship manufacturer Jinko-Tekina have supplied both the Argon Federation and Antigone Memorial with the Courier-class s-ship, designed for transporting small numbers of valuable wares across a short distance in order to limit the risk of hijacking as well as to share the load of M- and L-sized transporters that make up the rest of the Argon merchant fleet.
ship_arg_s_miner_solid_01_a.name: Courier (Mineral)

ship_arg_s_scout_01_a.description: Long serving as the scout and exploration ship for the Argon fleet, the Discoverer saw little re-design or overhaul until the Jump Gate shutdown. The current model, an overall improvement on the model used before the shutdown, was designed in the Antigone Republic and made available to the Argon Federation as part of an ongoing technology exchange between the two allied factions.
ship_arg_s_scout_01_a.name: Discoverer Vanguard

ship_arg_s_scout_01_b.description: Long serving as the scout and exploration ship for the Argon fleet, the Discoverer saw little re-design or overhaul until the Jump Gate shutdown. The current model, an overall improvement on the model used before the shutdown, was designed in the Antigone Republic and made available to the Argon Federation as part of an ongoing technology exchange between the two allied factions.
ship_arg_s_scout_01_b.name: Discoverer Sentinel

ship_arg_s_trans_container_01_a.description: After years of outcry from many a trader about the lack of a small and speedy transporter, the up and coming ship manufacturer Jinko-Tekina have supplied both the Argon Federation and Antigone Memorial with the Courier-class s-ship, designed for transporting small numbers of valuable wares across a short distance in order to limit the risk of hijacking as well as to share the load of M- and L-sized transporters that make up the rest of the Argon merchant fleet.
ship_arg_s_trans_container_01_a.name: Courier Vanguard

ship_arg_s_trans_container_01_b.description: After years of outcry from many a trader about the lack of a small and speedy transporter, the up and coming ship manufacturer Jinko-Tekina have supplied both the Argon Federation and Antigone Memorial with the Courier-class s-ship, designed for transporting small numbers of valuable wares across a short distance in order to limit the risk of hijacking as well as to share the load of M- and L-sized transporters that make up the rest of the Argon merchant fleet.
ship_arg_s_trans_container_01_b.name: Courier Sentinel

ship_arg_s_trans_container_02_a.description: While largely superseded by more modern courier designs, the Argon Callisto has a strange, cult attraction. In no area does this ship excel, and yet thousands of owners gather periodically at Hatikvah's Choice to show off their various paint jobs and module modifications.nnThe release of a Vanguard model did not make the ship materially more useful, however, Callisto aficionados do seem to appreciate it.
ship_arg_s_trans_container_02_a.name: Callisto Vanguard

ship_arg_s_trans_container_02_b.description: While largely superseded by more modern courier designs, the Argon Callisto has a strange, cult attraction. In no area does this ship excel, and yet thousands of owners gather periodically at Hatikvah's Choice to show off their various paint jobs and module modifications.nnThe release of a Sentinel variant is seen by some as evidence that the manufacturer has found a means of monetising the Callisto's enthusiastic fan base, rather than an attempt to build a performance spacecraft.
ship_arg_s_trans_container_02_b.name: Callisto Sentinel

ship_arg_xl_builder_01_a.description: With the method of building stations having changed numerous times since the beginning of the Jump Gate shutdown, many have questioned the necessity for a dedicated building vessel, but the Mammoth has continued to find purpose.nnUndergoing changes with every iteration of the station building method, the last iteration of the Mammoth saw it transformed into more of a mobile platform than a ship; becoming immobile when deployed to a station construction site so that construction drones can easily be ferried from the ship to the site and back again.
ship_arg_xl_builder_01_a.name: Mammoth Vanguard

ship_arg_xl_builder_01_b.description: With the method of building stations having changed numerous times since the beginning of the Jump Gate shutdown, many have questioned the necessity for a dedicated building vessel, but the Mammoth has continued to find purpose.nnUndergoing changes with every iteration of the station building method, the last iteration of the Mammoth saw it transformed into more of a mobile platform than a ship; becoming immobile when deployed to a station construction site so that construction drones can easily be ferried from the ship to the site and back again.
ship_arg_xl_builder_01_b.name: Mammoth Sentinel

ship_arg_xl_carrier_01_a.description: The final ship shared with the Argon Federation by the Antigone Republic as part of their ongoing technology exchange, the new-look Colossus-class carrier was initially designed as such so that it didn't use much-needed resources that were sorely lacking in Antigone Memorial during the Jump Gate shutdown.nnWith an array of medium and small docks and launch tubes, the Antigone Colossus is a significant improvement on other Colossus models, able to deploy ships to the battlefield at a much higher rate and collect and store them far more efficiently, at the cost of some of its previous firepower.
ship_arg_xl_carrier_01_a.name: Colossus Vanguard

ship_arg_xl_carrier_01_b.description: The final ship shared with the Argon Federation by the Antigone Republic as part of their ongoing technology exchange, the new-look Colossus-class carrier was initially designed as such so that it didn't use much-needed resources that were sorely lacking in Antigone Memorial during the Jump Gate shutdown.nnWith an array of medium and small docks and launch tubes, the Antigone Colossus is a significant improvement on other Colossus models, able to deploy ships to the battlefield at a much higher rate and collect and store them far more efficiently, at the cost of some of its previous firepower.
ship_arg_xl_carrier_01_b.name: Colossus Sentinel

ship_arg_xl_carrier_02_a.description: The final ship shared with the Argon Federation by the Antigone Republic as part of their ongoing technology exchange, the new-look Colossus-class carrier was initially designed as such so that it didn't use much-needed resources that were sorely lacking in Antigone Memorial during the Jump Gate shutdown.nnWith an array of medium and small docks and launch tubes, the Antigone Colossus is a significant improvement on other Colossus models, able to deploy ships to the battlefield at a much higher rate and collect and store them far more efficiently, at the cost of some of its previous firepower.nnThe E-model variation was introduced at a later date, and updated the superficial design to bring it up to par with Commonwealth standards that were established during the period of disconnection. The precursor version is slowly being phased out, but will remain available for interested third parties.
ship_arg_xl_carrier_02_a.name: Colossus E

ship_arg_xl_resupplier_01_a.description: Very quickly developed during the course of the latest defence against the Xenon, the Nomad is effectively a mobile equipment dock, allowing combat ships to repair and restock and get back to the fight more quickly. This has greatly helped the efficiency of both the Argon Federation and Antigone Republic fleets, and has already begun to see clones created across both Teladi and Paranid space.nnThe ship is not self-sufficient, requiring a defensive escort not to be torn apart in combat. If it can be defended though, the Nomad can prove a devastating support ship.
ship_arg_xl_resupplier_01_a.name: Nomad Vanguard

ship_arg_xl_resupplier_01_b.description: Very quickly developed during the course of the latest defence against the Xenon, the Nomad is effectively a mobile equipment dock, allowing combat ships to repair and restock and get back to the fight more quickly. This has greatly helped the efficiency of both the Argon Federation and Antigone Republic fleets, and has already begun to see clones created across both Teladi and Paranid space.nnThe ship is not self-sufficient, requiring a defensive escort not to be torn apart in combat. If it can be defended though, the Nomad can prove a devastating support ship.
ship_arg_xl_resupplier_01_b.name: Nomad Sentinel

ship_arg_xs_police_01_a.description: Argon Station Security Vessels police the vicinity of Argon installations, seeking out felons and those carrying contraband. While they are lightly armed, crossing them may compromise a captain's good standing with the authorities.
ship_arg_xs_police_01_a.name: Argon Station Security Vessel

ship_gen_m_transdrone_container_01_a.description: The Drop Drone is an essential system in terraforming operations, transporting the required surface-bound wares.nnThe Medium-sized model is an upgraded drone with additional capacity.
ship_gen_m_transdrone_container_01_a.name: Medium Drop Drone

ship_gen_m_tugboat_01_a.description: With the advent of the Dark, Avarice was forced to rely on a fleet of stranded ships that had been taken by surprise by the Jump Gate shutdown. Despite the almost immediate collapse of the Avarice company, capable scientists and engineers soon took it upon themselves to stabilise the system and work towards sustainability. They first established a functioning wharf for repairs and replacement, and conducted early experiments into developing a recycling economy in anticipation of dwindling resources within the system. The regeneration of this post-shutdown economy was quickly curtailed by the first Tides, which destroyed most stations and ships in the system in 800 (NT). However, a side-effect of the destructive solar event was the greatly increased energy output from solar panels, which allowed for a paradigm shift. The new Rakers economy emerging from Tidebreak, at the time the only station in Avarice, took advantage of this new abundance of energy. This, along with the use of local wrecks, enabled the rebuilding of a new, sustainable, closed-loop economy based on recycling, allowing them to further improve on previous ship and station module designs. Resettlement of Avarice began in 818 (NT), when the Northriver Company introduced Protectyon and the associated Protectyon Shield Generators to safeguard stations from the Tide. A few years later, in 825 (NT), the reconnection of Windfall gave the long-isolated Rakers the opportunity to trade with various Syndicate factions, but also exposed them to their piracy.nnWith the Jump Gate shutdown disconnecting Avarice the first Tug ship, a predecessor of the Manticore, was quickly developed by stranded Argon engineers to tug small ship wrecks to stations for further processing and make best use of the now very limited access to resources. With just one of those ships and their first version of the Scrap Processor and Scrap Recycler modules they ran a small enterprise with a high demand for energy and a slow output of Hull Parts and Claytronics. It was only after the first Tide destroyed most stations and ships in the system in 800 (NT) that their blueprints became the main pillar of Rakers economy and were further developed into the now widely used Manticore, utilising more powerful engines, and station modules. After messenger drones from Sacred Relic arrived, the people of Avarice themselves sent out messenger drones containing the blueprints to other disconnected systems, hoping to help those unfortunate enough to be stranded with no natural resources in reach. In doing this they created a local branch of the Alliance of the Word operating from Tidebreak. With the first Jump gate reconnection in 825 (NT) the Alliance of the Word would move to an own station in Windfall.
ship_gen_m_tugboat_01_a.name: Manticore

ship_gen_s_fighter_01_a.description: Perhaps the most interesting thing about the Nodan fighter is the lack of information available regarding its existence. It is only known to be available from the Alliance of the Word, and the organisation has remained tight-lipped as to the vessel's inception and design.nnThe design is somewhat quirky, with no clear antecedence related to other faction architecture. However, the Nodan has earned itself plaudits from committed dogfighters, based on its impressive speed, agility and deceleration.nnFor those seeking additional speed, the Nodan Vanguard model is available.
ship_gen_s_fighter_01_a.name: Nodan Vanguard

ship_gen_s_fighter_01_b.description: Perhaps the most interesting thing about the Nodan fighter is the lack of information available regarding its existence. It is only known to be available from the Alliance of the Word, and the organisation has remained tight-lipped as to the vessel's inception and design.nnThe design is somewhat quirky, with no clear antecedence related to other faction architecture. However, the Nodan has earned itself plaudits from committed dogfighters, based on its impressive speed, agility and deceleration.nnThe Sentinel model provides the ship with a hardened superstructure.
ship_gen_s_fighter_01_b.name: Nodan Sentinel

ship_gen_s_fightingdrone_01_a.description: Advanced battlefield tactical command has been transformed by the evolution of Defence Drones. Once little more than cannon fodder, a drone swarm is now a formidable defensive capability.nnThe very best commanders are able to coordinate drone and non-drone strategies, adapting them to make best use of the strengths of each. Defence drone mastery is not an easy task, however; it is the mark of a top-flight tactician.
ship_gen_s_fightingdrone_01_a.name: Defence Drone

ship_gen_s_lasertower_01_a.description: Deployed from a compartment of a ship, the laser tower is a static combat unit used primarily in the defence of an area of objective. They use a simple targeting system to turn towards the enemy before firing. Though laser towers can target and hit small ships, they are most effective at medium range against medium or larger targets.
ship_gen_s_lasertower_01_a.name: Laser Tower Mk2

ship_gen_s_miningdrone_liquid_01_a.description: The Gas Collector Drone is an advanced drone system that greatly increases the efficiency of gaseous harvesting operations, particularly when used in large quantities.
ship_gen_s_miningdrone_liquid_01_a.name: Gas Collector Drone

ship_gen_s_miningdrone_solid_01_a.description: The humble Mining Drone is an essential asset at the heart of any primary resource-based business empire. Inexpensive, but highly vulnerable, the "termite" as it is colloquially referred to, is highly effective, particularly when deployed in large numbers.
ship_gen_s_miningdrone_solid_01_a.name: Mining Drone

ship_gen_s_transdrone_container_01_a.description: The Drop Drone is an essential system in terraforming operations, transporting the required surface-bound wares.
ship_gen_s_transdrone_container_01_a.name: Small Drop Drone

ship_gen_xs_buildingdrone_01_a.description: Affectionately named "termites", building drones are an essential component of station construction.nnAll races have moved to drone-based construction due to the scale of injury and loss of sentient life incurred through manual methods.
ship_gen_xs_buildingdrone_01_a.name: Building Drone

ship_gen_xs_cargodrone_empty_01_a.description: The Cargo Drone is a Teladi invention that expedites freight transfers and is ubiquitously popular among merchants across the known galaxy.
ship_gen_xs_cargodrone_empty_01_a.name: Cargo Drone

ship_gen_xs_lasertower_01_a.description: Deployed from a compartment of a ship, the laser tower is a static combat unit used primarily in the defence of an area of objective. They use a simple targeting system to turn towards the enemy before firing. Though laser towers can target and hit small ships, they are most effective at medium range against medium or larger targets.
ship_gen_xs_lasertower_01_a.name: Laser Tower Mk1

ship_gen_xs_repairdrone_01_a.description: The Repair Drone is an autonomous, self-propelled micro-vessel that is used for the repair of ship and station hulls.
ship_gen_xs_repairdrone_01_a.name: Repair Drone

ship_kha_l_destroyer_01_a.description: While once little more than a rumour, sightings of Ravagers are becoming increasingly more frequent. Little is known about this ship class. However, thanks to its deadly primary weapon, it is able to deal massive amounts of damage to any that come across it. As the command hierarchy of the Kha'ak is pretty much impossible to discern, it is unknown precisely how the Ravager functions within the overall Kha'ak structure, but it is generally agreed that it appears to be acting as a capital ship.
ship_kha_l_destroyer_01_a.name: Ravager

ship_kha_m_fighter_01_a.description: Armed with the frighteningly accurate Kyon Emitter, the Kha'ak Queen's Guard most commonly attacks in swarm formation to devastating effect.nnThe pseudo-insectile nature of the Kha'ak gave rise to the theory that they are directly or indirectly controlled by a Queen. This, in turn, inspired the name given to this vessel. However, the theory is as yet unverified.
ship_kha_m_fighter_01_a.name: Queen's Guard

ship_kha_m_fighter_02_a.description: The diabolical pyramids of the Kha'ak are a perpetual threat. The Hive Guard is a heavy fighter and one of the more disturbing Kha'ak ships that may appear on a pilot's scanner.
ship_kha_m_fighter_02_a.name: Hive Guard

ship_kha_s_fighter_01_a.description: The appearance of the Kha'ak can trigger a sinking feeling in the stomach or stomachs of any pilot, however accomplished. These "space bugs", as they are often described, attack in mystifyingly geometric vessels, and have, thus far, rebuffed all attempts at communication.nnThis fighter has been dubbed the Protector and, like all Kha'ak ships, it is well-armed and trigger-happy.
ship_kha_s_fighter_01_a.name: Protector

ship_kha_s_fighter_02_a.description: The Kha'ak Forager is a lightning fast and highly manoeuvrable scout ship, with little accompanying weaponry. While notionally fragile, Commonwealth pilots report that the Forager is notoriously hard to catch and target.
ship_kha_s_fighter_02_a.name: Forager

ship_kha_xl_battleship_01_a.description: This monstrosity has only been spotted a few times throughout history, before disappearing never to reappear again. It is unknown whether there is just a single Obliterator, or whether there are many more of them, lurking outside of currently explored space. The Obliterator is a hulking behemoth that is best compared to a battleship, although these comparisons break down when discussing the organic constructions of the Kha'ak. Very little is known about the Obliterator, as it is rare that anything survives an encounter with this ship. The fear that it causes is understandable, since on several occasions it has turned its devastating power on a planet, using its weapons to obliterate that planet entirely.
ship_kha_xl_battleship_01_a.name: Obliterator

ship_par_l_destroyer_01_a.description: The Paranid Odysseus is a highly regarded destroyer with an often consequential presence in large scale engagements.nnThe latest version's architect is the notoriously sensitive Olmanckabssit, who is reputed to fly into a rage at the slightest mention of the vessel's resemblance to a hair dryer.nnThe Odysseus Vanguard has enhanced speed.
ship_par_l_destroyer_01_a.name: Odysseus Vanguard

ship_par_l_destroyer_01_b.description: The Paranid Odysseus is a highly regarded destroyer with an often consequential presence in large scale engagements.nnThe latest version's architect is the notoriously sensitive Olmanckabssit, who is reputed to fly into a rage at the slightest mention of the vessel's resemblance to a hair dryer.nnThe Sentinel model boasts greater resilience.
ship_par_l_destroyer_01_b.name: Odysseus Sentinel

ship_par_l_destroyer_02_a.description: The Paranid Odysseus is a highly regarded destroyer with an often consequential presence in large scale engagements.nnThe E-model variation was introduced at a later date, and updated the superficial design to bring it up to par with Commonwealth standards that were established during the period of disconnection. The precursor version is slowly being phased out, but will remain available for interested third parties.nnThe Odysseus E-model prioritises hardpoints, however, it suffers from reduced storage capacity as a consequence.
ship_par_l_destroyer_02_a.name: Odysseus E

ship_par_l_miner_liquid_01_a.description: The Chthonios miner boasts impressive speed. The economics of running a large production empire require the optimisation of miners' speed versus cargo space. With sufficient speed, a constant stream of smaller deliveries of resources can keep mega-factories running more efficiently than if they are left waiting for larger shipments.nnThe Chthonios is tailored for just this style of "just-in-time" logistics management, and is available in models able to collect gases or minerals. The Vanguard variant adds even more speed.
ship_par_l_miner_liquid_01_a.name: Chthonios (Gas) Vanguard

ship_par_l_miner_liquid_01_b.description: The Chthonios miner boasts impressive speed. The economics of running a large production empire require the optimisation of miners' speed versus cargo space. With sufficient speed, a constant stream of smaller deliveries of resources can keep mega-factories running more efficiently than if they are left waiting for larger shipments.nnThe Chthonios is tailored for just this style of "just-in-time" logistics management, and is available in models able to collect gases or minerals. The Sentinel variant enhances structural robustness in case miners encounter hostiles.
ship_par_l_miner_liquid_01_b.name: Chthonios (Gas) Sentinel

ship_par_l_miner_liquid_02_a.description: The Chthonios miner boasts impressive speed. The economics of running a large production empire require the optimisation of miners' speed versus cargo space. With sufficient speed, a constant stream of smaller deliveries of resources can keep mega-factories running more efficiently than if they are left waiting for larger shipments.nnThe Chthonios is tailored for just this style of "just-in-time" logistics management, and is available in models able to collect gases or minerals.nnThe E-model variation was introduced at a later date, and updated the superficial design to bring it up to par with Commonwealth standards that were established during the period of disconnection. The precursor version is slowly being phased out, but will remain available for interested third parties.
ship_par_l_miner_liquid_02_a.name: Chthonios E (Gas)

ship_par_l_miner_solid_01_a.description: The Chthonios miner boasts impressive speed. The economics of running a large production empire require the optimisation of miners' speed versus cargo space. With sufficient speed, a constant stream of smaller deliveries of resources can keep mega-factories running more efficiently than if they are left waiting for larger shipments.nnThe Chthonios is tailored for just this style of "just-in-time" logistics management, and is available in models able to collect gases or minerals. The Vanguard variant adds even more speed.
ship_par_l_miner_solid_01_a.name: Chthonios (Mineral) Vanguard

ship_par_l_miner_solid_01_b.description: The Chthonios miner boasts impressive speed. The economics of running a large production empire require the optimisation of miners' speed versus cargo space. With sufficient speed, a constant stream of smaller deliveries of resources can keep mega-factories running more efficiently than if they are left waiting for larger shipments.nnThe Chthonios is tailored for just this style of "just-in-time" logistics management, and is available in models able to collect gases or minerals. The Sentinel variant enhances structural robustness in case miners encounter hostiles.
ship_par_l_miner_solid_01_b.name: Chthonios (Mineral) Sentinel

ship_par_l_miner_solid_02_a.description: The Chthonios miner boasts impressive speed. The economics of running a large production empire require the optimisation of miners' speed versus cargo space. With sufficient speed, a constant stream of smaller deliveries of resources can keep mega-factories running more efficiently than if they are left waiting for larger shipments.nnThe Chthonios is tailored for just this style of "just-in-time" logistics management, and is available in models able to collect gases or minerals.nnThe E-model variation was introduced at a later date, and updated the superficial design to bring it up to par with Commonwealth standards that were established during the period of disconnection. The precursor version is slowly being phased out, but will remain available for interested third parties.
ship_par_l_miner_solid_02_a.name: Chthonios E (Mineral)
